---
title: "Feeding & Respiration"
author: "Raz"
date: "27-01-2026"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    theme: lumen
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    df_print: paged
    toc_float:
    
      
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: false



---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)



## Data preparation and general setup
### Global setup: packages, helper functions, and plotting conventions

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(reshape2)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)
library(gridExtra)
library(hms)
library(lubridate)
library(cowplot)
#install.packages("googlesheets4")
library(googlesheets4)
library(epitools)
library(openair)
library(ggpubr)
#install.packages("slider")   # פעם אחת
library(slider)              # בכל סשן
library(pracma)
library(zoo)


#Rmarkdown assembly
library(rmdformats)
library(rmarkdown)
library(knitr)
library(formattable)
library(systemfonts)
library(kableExtra)
library(tinytex) #if this installation does not work, read section below in RStudio 

# Force dplyr versions of commonly masked verbs
select   <- dplyr::select
filter   <- dplyr::filter
mutate   <- dplyr::mutate
summarise<- dplyr::summarise
arrange  <- dplyr::arrange



```


## Data import + minimal preprocessing (canonical base table)
## Timestamps + Date + chronological sort (NO Feeding propagation)

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
## =============================================================================
## Data import + minimal preprocessing (canonical base table)
## Timestamps + Date + chronological sort (NO Feeding propagation)
## =============================================================================
## Purpose:
##   - Define project output folder once (out_dir)
##   - Read raw CSV once
##   - Parse Date_and_time (POSIXct), derive Date (day), sort chronologically
##   - Keep Feeding labels as-is (no imputation/propagation)
##   - Save a single “base” dataset for all downstream analyses:
##       (1) RDS for reproducibility/speed
##       (2) CSV snapshot for transparency/sharing
## =============================================================================

library(dplyr)

# -------------------------
# 0) Paths (single source of truth)
# -------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

in_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/Work 2020/Feeding & Respiration/Firesting files - Sponge/F&R Data Base 09-08-2023/F&R/Sep_2024"
in_csv <- file.path(in_dir, "FNR_DATA_17.09.2024.csv")

# -------------------------
# 1) Read raw data
# -------------------------
iv_feeding_base <- read.csv(in_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# -------------------------
# 2) Minimal preprocessing: time parsing + Date + sort
# -------------------------
iv_feeding_base$Date_and_time <- as.POSIXct(
  iv_feeding_base$Date_and_time,
  format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
)

# Derive Date from Date_and_time (safer than trusting an imported Date column)
iv_feeding_base$Date <- as.Date(iv_feeding_base$Date_and_time)

iv_feeding_base <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  arrange(Date_and_time)

# -------------------------
# 3) Sanity checks
# -------------------------
cat("Rows:", nrow(iv_feeding_base), "\n")
cat("Time range:", range(iv_feeding_base$Date_and_time, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Feeding counts (raw, unchanged):\n")
print(table(iv_feeding_base$Feeding, useNA = "ifany"))

# -------------------------
# 4) Save base dataset (recommended for the whole chapter)
# -------------------------
rds_file <- file.path(out_dir, "iv_feeding_base.rds")
csv_file <- file.path(out_dir, "iv_feeding_base.csv")

saveRDS(iv_feeding_base, rds_file)
write.csv(iv_feeding_base, csv_file, row.names = FALSE)

cat("Saved:\n", rds_file, "\n", csv_file, "\n")

# -------------------------
# 5) Backward compatibility (optional)
#    If old chunks still reference Sep_2024_Data, keep this alias.
#    Delete later once you've renamed everywhere.
# -------------------------
Sep_2024_Data <- iv_feeding_base


```


## Summary tables for Chapter 2 (OFR, ΔO2, Respiration)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
# =========================================================
# Summary tables for Chapter 2 (OFR, ΔO2, Respiration)
# Two levels:
#   (1) Osculum-level: stats within each Osculum_ID (nested in sponge)
#   (2) Sponge-level: stats computed FROM osculum-level means (each osculum = 1 unit)
#
# Output (6 tables total):
#   OFR_osculum,   OFR_sponge
#   dO2_osculum,   dO2_sponge
#   Resp_osculum,  Resp_sponge
#
# Each table contains:
#   n, mean, median, sd, se, ci95, cv, min, max
#
# Notes:
# - Sponge-level tables are computed from osculum means (avoids overweighting dense time series).
# - Uses raw columns as-is (no Feeding propagation here).
# - ΔO2 column name in your data is assumed to be `ΔO2` (with the delta symbol).
# =========================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

# -------------------------
# 0) Helpers (robust summary stats)
# -------------------------

# Core summary stats (returns one-row tibble)
summ_stats <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)

  if (n == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      n = 0,
      mean = NA_real_,
      median = NA_real_,
      sd = NA_real_,
      se = NA_real_,
      ci95 = NA_real_,
      cv = NA_real_,
      min = NA_real_,
      max = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  m   <- mean(x)
  med <- median(x)
  mn  <- min(x)
  mx  <- max(x)

  if (n < 2) {
    return(tibble(
      n = n,
      mean = m,
      median = med,
      sd = NA_real_,
      se = NA_real_,
      ci95 = NA_real_,
      cv = NA_real_,
      min = mn,
      max = mx
    ))
  }

  s     <- sd(x)
  se    <- s / sqrt(n)
  tcrit <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1)
  ci    <- tcrit * se

  # CV (%) guarded against mean ~ 0
  cv <- ifelse(is.finite(m) && m != 0, 100 * (s / abs(m)), NA_real_)

  tibble(
    n = n,
    mean = m,
    median = med,
    sd = s,
    se = se,
    ci95 = ci,
    cv = cv,
    min = mn,
    max = mx
  )
}

# -------------------------
# 1) Base dataset (IDs only)
# -------------------------
# Assumes Sep_2024_Data already exists in the environment.
# If you're using the new canonical object name, replace Sep_2024_Data with iv_feeding_base.

df_base <- Sep_2024_Data %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod  = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Repeat_Code = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID  = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Repeat_Code), !is.na(Sponge_cod), !is.na(Osculum_ID))

# Quick check
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_base), "\n")
cat("Sponges:", n_distinct(df_base$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("Osculum series:", n_distinct(df_base$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# =========================================================
# 2) Osculum-level tables (unit = Osculum_ID)
#    Stats computed across repeated measurements within each osculum series.
# =========================================================

# ---------- OFR (osculum-level) ----------
OFR_osculum <- df_base %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(OFR),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID)

# ---------- ΔO2 (osculum-level) ----------
dO2_osculum <- df_base %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(`ΔO2`),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID)

# ---------- Respiration (osculum-level) ----------
Resp_osculum <- df_base %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(Respiration),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID)

# =========================================================
# 3) Sponge-level tables (unit = Osculum_ID mean)
#    IMPORTANT:
#    Each osculum contributes ONE value (its mean), so sponges are summarized
#    from the distribution of osculum means (equal weight per osculum).
#
#    Column names are kept SIMPLE and IDENTICAL to osculum-level tables:
#    n, mean, median, sd, se, ci95, cv, min, max
# =========================================================

# ---------- OFR (sponge-level; computed from osculum means) ----------
OFR_sponge <- OFR_osculum %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(mean),   # mean here = osculum-level mean OFR
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod)

# ---------- ΔO2 (sponge-level; computed from osculum means) ----------
dO2_sponge <- dO2_osculum %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(mean),   # mean here = osculum-level mean ΔO2
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod)

# ---------- Respiration (sponge-level; computed from osculum means) ----------
Resp_sponge <- Resp_osculum %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod) %>%
  summarise(
    summ_stats(mean),   # mean here = osculum-level mean Respiration
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod)

# =========================================================
# 4) Optional: rounded versions (for supplement / copy-paste)
# =========================================================
OFR_osculum_round  <- OFR_osculum  %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))
OFR_sponge_round   <- OFR_sponge   %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))

dO2_osculum_round  <- dO2_osculum  %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))
dO2_sponge_round   <- dO2_sponge   %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))

Resp_osculum_round <- Resp_osculum %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))
Resp_sponge_round  <- Resp_sponge  %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2)))

# =========================================================
# 5) Export (6 tables + optional rounded)
# =========================================================
# Uses your chapter output folder (set once elsewhere in your script)
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Raw (full precision)
write.csv(OFR_osculum,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_OFR_osculum_level.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(OFR_sponge,   file.path(out_dir, "Table_OFR_sponge_level.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(dO2_osculum,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_dO2_osculum_level.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dO2_sponge,   file.path(out_dir, "Table_dO2_sponge_level.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(Resp_osculum, file.path(out_dir, "Table_Resp_osculum_level.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(Resp_sponge,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_Resp_sponge_level.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)

# Rounded (optional)
write.csv(OFR_osculum_round,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_OFR_osculum_level_ROUND.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(OFR_sponge_round,   file.path(out_dir, "Table_OFR_sponge_level_ROUND.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(dO2_osculum_round,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_dO2_osculum_level_ROUND.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dO2_sponge_round,   file.path(out_dir, "Table_dO2_sponge_level_ROUND.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(Resp_osculum_round, file.path(out_dir, "Table_Resp_osculum_level_ROUND.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(Resp_sponge_round,  file.path(out_dir, "Table_Resp_sponge_level_ROUND.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)




```
## Osculum flow rate (OFR)
### Supplementary figure: OFR time series + feeding lines
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

# =========================================================
# Supplementary figure: OFR time series + feeding lines
# =========================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(scales)

# -------------------------
# 0) Output folder
# -------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"

# -------------------------
# 1) Plot settings
# -------------------------
tz_def <- "UTC"  # change only if timestamps are local

sponge_palette <- c(
  "82" = "#FF69B4",
  "83" = "#8B3A62",
  "84" = "#E066FF",
  "85" = "#7A378B",
  "86" = "#1C86EE",
  "87" = "#104E8B"
)

# Feeding times (vertical lines)
feed_times <- as.POSIXct(c(
  "2020-12-08 13:51:00","2020-12-09 16:49:00","2020-12-10 16:38:00","2020-12-11 12:18:00",
  "2020-12-23 11:30:00","2020-12-24 13:20:00","2020-12-26 14:44:00","2020-12-30 11:36:00",
  "2020-12-31 16:53:00","2021-01-01 17:50:00","2021-01-07 17:28:00","2021-01-08 12:40:00",
  "2021-01-09 16:38:00","2021-01-13 16:07:00","2021-01-14 16:15:00","2021-01-15 13:27:00",
  "2021-01-20 11:06:00","2021-01-21 12:02:00","2021-01-22 15:30:00","2021-01-27 13:12:00",
  "2021-01-28 16:09:00","2021-01-29 13:01:00"
), tz = tz_def)

feed_df <- tibble(feed_time = feed_times)

# -------------------------
# 2) Data prep (OFR only)
# -------------------------
dat_ofr <- iv_feeding_base %>%   # canonical base dataset
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time, tz = tz_def),
    OFR           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR)),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Date_and_time), is.finite(OFR), !is.na(Sponge_cod))

# -------------------------
# 3) Figure: OFR time series + feeding lines
# -------------------------
FigS_OFR_timeseries <- ggplot(dat_ofr, aes(x = Date_and_time, y = OFR, color = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(
    data = feed_df, aes(xintercept = feed_time),
    colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = sponge_palette, guide = "none") +
  
  # ---- X axis: explicit time scale ----
  scale_x_datetime(
    breaks = date_breaks("3 days"),
    labels = date_format("%d-%b\n%H:%M"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = expression("OFR (L "*h^{-1}*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.border     = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 10))

FigS_OFR_timeseries

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "FigS_OFR_timeseries_feeding_lines.png"),
  plot     = FigS_OFR_timeseries,
  width    = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300
)


```
### Between-sponge differences in OFR:
### Osculum-level means (ANOVA and Kruskal–Wallis, for comparison) 
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
# ==================================================================================================
# Between-sponge differences in OFR (osculum-level means as analytical units)
#
# NOTE:
#   This block intentionally includes a classic one-way ANOVA and assumption checks.
#   Although ANOVA was ultimately NOT used for inference (see Results),
#   it is retained here for transparency and methodological completeness.
#   Final inference is based on Kruskal–Wallis.
#
# Analytical unit:
#   One row = one Osculum_ID (i.e., osculum-level time series)
# Response:
#   mean OFR within each Osculum_ID (column: mean)
# Grouping factor:
#   Sponge_cod (individual sponge)
# ==================================================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(car)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Input (NEW TABLE NAMES)
#    Expected table: OFR_osculum
#    Expected columns: Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID, mean
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("OFR_osculum"))
stopifnot(all(c("Sponge_cod", "Osculum_ID", "mean") %in% names(OFR_osculum)))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Build analysis dataset (one row per Osculum_ID)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
dat_aov <- OFR_osculum %>%
  filter(is.finite(mean)) %>%
  transmute(
    Sponge_cod  = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Osculum_ID  = factor(Osculum_ID),
    OFR_mean_os = as.numeric(mean)   # rename internally for readability
  )

# sanity
message("n sponges: ", n_distinct(dat_aov$Sponge_cod))
message("n oscula (analytical units): ", n_distinct(dat_aov$Osculum_ID))
print(table(dat_aov$Sponge_cod))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Classic one-way ANOVA (kept for transparency, not final inference)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
m_aov <- aov(OFR_mean_os ~ Sponge_cod, data = dat_aov)

cat("\n============================\n")
cat("ONE-WAY ANOVA (classic)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(summary(m_aov))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Assumption checks (diagnostics + tests)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3a) Diagnostic plots (inspect visually)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(m_aov, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted")
plot(m_aov, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# 3b) Normality of residuals (Shapiro; sensitive with moderate n)
shap <- shapiro.test(residuals(m_aov))
cat("\n============================\n")
cat("Residual normality (Shapiro)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(shap)

# 3c) Equal variances (Levene)
lev <- leveneTest(OFR_mean_os ~ Sponge_cod, data = dat_aov)
cat("\n============================\n")
cat("Equal variances (Levene)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(lev)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Robust alternatives
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4a) Welch ANOVA (variance-robust)
welch <- oneway.test(OFR_mean_os ~ Sponge_cod, data = dat_aov, var.equal = FALSE)
cat("\n============================\n")
cat("Welch one-way test (variance-robust)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(welch)

# 4b) Kruskal–Wallis (nonparametric; FINAL inference)
kw <- kruskal.test(OFR_mean_os ~ Sponge_cod, data = dat_aov)
cat("\n============================\n")
cat("Kruskal–Wallis (FINAL inference)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(kw)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Compact results (for copy/paste into Results)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
aov_summary <- summary(m_aov)[[1]]
p_lev   <- lev[1, "Pr(>F)"]
p_shap  <- shap$p.value

res_out <- list(
  n_sponges = n_distinct(dat_aov$Sponge_cod),
  n_oscula  = n_distinct(dat_aov$Osculum_ID),

  ANOVA_F   = unname(aov_summary["Sponge_cod", "F value"]),
  ANOVA_df1 = unname(aov_summary["Sponge_cod", "Df"]),
  ANOVA_df2 = unname(aov_summary["Residuals", "Df"]),
  ANOVA_p   = unname(aov_summary["Sponge_cod", "Pr(>F)"]),

  Levene_p  = as.numeric(p_lev),
  Shapiro_p = as.numeric(p_shap),

  Welch_p   = welch$p.value,

  KW_chi2   = unname(kw$statistic),
  KW_df     = unname(kw$parameter),
  KW_p      = kw$p.value
)

cat("\n============================\n")
cat("COMPACT RESULTS (for text)\n")
cat("============================\n")
print(res_out)


```



### OFR diel analysis (24 h): GAMs + extract numbers for Results text
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

# ==================================================================================================
# OFR diel analysis (24h): fit + plot + extract numbers for Results
# Canonical version (does NOT overwrite base tables)
# ==================================================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)

# -------------------------
# 0) Inputs (canonical)
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base") | exists("Sep_2024_Data"))

df0 <- if (exists("iv_feeding_base")) iv_feeding_base else Sep_2024_Data

# output folder (single source of truth)
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# 1) Build diel dataset (OFR only; no feeding alignment)
# -------------------------
# Robust osculum identifier: prefer Repeat_Code if present, else Repeat_Index
rep_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Repeat_Code"  %in% names(df0) ~ "Repeat_Code",
  "Repeat_Index" %in% names(df0) ~ "Repeat_Index",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(rep_col)) stop("Need Repeat_Code or Repeat_Index to construct Osculum_ID.")

df_ofr <- df0 %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),  # should already be POSIXct in base
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Rep_id        = as.character(.data[[rep_col]]),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Rep_id, sep = "_")),
    hour          = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")),
    DayNight      = factor(ifelse(hour >= 6 & hour < 18, "Day", "Night"),
                           levels = c("Day", "Night")),
    tod           = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")) +
                    as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%M"))/60 +
                    as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%S"))/3600
  ) %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(OFR),
    !is.na(Date_and_time),
    is.finite(tod), tod >= 0, tod < 24,
    !is.na(Sponge_cod),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("Diel OFR dataset:\n")
cat("n obs =", nrow(df_ofr), "\n")
cat("n sponges =", n_distinct(df_ofr$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("n oscula  =", n_distinct(df_ofr$Osculum_ID), "\n")
print(table(df_ofr$Sponge_cod))
print(table(df_ofr$DayNight))

# -------------------------
# 2) Fit models
# -------------------------
# A) population cyclic smooth
m_ofr_tod <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8),
  data   = df_ofr,
  method = "REML",
  knots  = list(tod = c(0, 24))
)

# B) binary day/night as LM (so you get Estimate ± SE, t, p exactly like in Results)
m_ofr_dn_lm <- lm(OFR ~ DayNight, data = df_ofr)

# C) population cyclic + sponge-specific deviations (factor-smooth interaction)
m_ofr_sponge <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8) + s(tod, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data   = df_ofr,
  method = "REML",
  knots  = list(tod = c(0, 24))
)

# -------------------------
# 3) Extract numbers for Results text
# -------------------------
extract_smooth <- function(mod, pattern) {
  sm <- summary(mod)
  st <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  st$term <- rownames(st); rownames(st) <- NULL
  pcol <- intersect(names(st), c("p-value", "p.value"))
  if (length(pcol) == 1) names(st)[names(st) == pcol] <- "p_value"

  i <- grep(pattern, st$term)
  if (length(i) == 0) return(data.frame(term=NA, edf=NA, F=NA, p=NA))
  i <- i[1]
  data.frame(
    term = st$term[i],
    edf  = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
    F    = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
    p    = as.numeric(st$p_value[i])
  )
}

A_sum <- summary(m_ofr_tod)
C_sum <- summary(m_ofr_sponge)

A_s_tod <- extract_smooth(m_ofr_tod, "s\\(tod\\)")
C_s_tod <- extract_smooth(m_ofr_sponge, "s\\(tod\\)")
C_s_fs  <- extract_smooth(m_ofr_sponge, "s\\(tod,Sponge_cod\\)")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL A (population cyclic smooth)\n============================\n")
cat("s(tod): edf=", round(A_s_tod$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(A_s_tod$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(A_s_tod$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("adjusted R2=", round(A_sum$r.sq, 4),
    "; deviance explained=", round(100*A_sum$dev.expl, 2), "%\n", sep="")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL B (Day vs Night; LM)\n============================\n")
lm_sum <- summary(m_ofr_dn_lm)
coef_tab <- coef(lm_sum)
dn_row <- grep("^DayNight", rownames(coef_tab))
if (length(dn_row) > 0) {
  dn_row <- dn_row[1]
  cat("DayNight effect: estimate=",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"Estimate"], 2), " ± ",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"Std. Error"], 2), " SE, t=",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"t value"], 2), ", p=",
      signif(coef_tab[dn_row,"Pr(>|t|)"], 3), "\n", sep="")
} else {
  cat("DayNight coefficient not found (check factor coding).\n")
}
cat("R2=", round(lm_sum$r.squared, 4),
    "; adjusted R2=", round(lm_sum$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n", sep="")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL C (population + sponge-specific deviations)\n============================\n")
cat("global s(tod): edf=", round(C_s_tod$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(C_s_tod$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(C_s_tod$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("sponge-specific s(tod,Sponge_cod): edf=", round(C_s_fs$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(C_s_fs$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(C_s_fs$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("adjusted R2=", round(C_sum$r.sq, 4),
    "; deviance explained=", round(100*C_sum$dev.expl, 2), "%\n", sep="")

# -------------------------
# 4) Figure (A|B) with fixed sponge colors (gratia)
# -------------------------
library(gratia)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# your canonical palette for the whole chapter
sponge_palette <- c(
  "82" = "#FF69B4",
  "83" = "#8B3A62",
  "84" = "#E066FF",
  "85" = "#7A378B",
  "86" = "#1C86EE",
  "87" = "#104E8B"
)

# A: population smooth
pA <- draw(
  m_ofr_tod,
  select = "s(tod)",
  rug = FALSE
) +
  labs(
    title = "A",
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Partial effect on OFR"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, 6), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

# B: sponge-specific deviations (colored)
pB <- draw(
  m_ofr_sponge,
  select = "s(tod,Sponge_cod)",
  se = FALSE,
  rug = FALSE
) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = sponge_palette, name = "Sponge") +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, 6), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

p_combined <- (pA | pB) + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_OFR_diel_GAM_population_and_sponge.png"),
  plot     = p_combined,
  width    = 12,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

p_combined


```


### OFR response to feeding (0–12 h): dataset construction + GAM workflow + key outputs + QA
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# OFR response to feeding (0–12 h): dataset construction + GAM workflow + key outputs + QA
#
# PURPOSE
#   Create one canonical, feeding-aligned dataset for OFR in I. variabilis (0–12 h post-feeding),
#   and run a matched set of GAMs to test whether OFR changes with time since feeding.
#
# UNIT OF ANALYSIS
#   Time series = Osculum_ID = Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code
#   Feed_time   = first timestamp within each Osculum_ID where Feeding == "after"
#
# OUTPUTS
#   1) Canonical dataset: df_ofr_12h  (used for plots + models + QA)
#   2) GAM models (REML): m0–m4 (increasing structure)
#   3) Model comparisons: anova.gam (F-tests) + AIC table
#   4) Compact “key numbers” printout for Results text
#   5) Main figure: partial effects (Panel A global smooth, Panel B sponge-specific deviations)
#   6) QA checks: alignment (bin 0), coverage (bins), duplicates (timestamps/bins)
#
# ASSUMPTIONS / REQUIREMENTS
#   - iv_feeding_base exists (created in your import/prep chunk)
#   - Columns used here exist: Taxa_cod, Date_and_time, Feeding, OFR, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code
#   - out_dir exists (or you define it below)
# ==================================================================================================

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Libraries + fixed palette (chapter-wide)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(dplyr)

cols <- c(
  "82" = "#FF69B4",
  "83" = "#8B3A62",
  "84" = "#E066FF",
  "85" = "#7A378B",
  "86" = "#1C86EE",
  "87" = "#104E8B"
)

# Path for saving figures (set once per chapter; safe fallback here)
if (!exists("out_dir")) {
  out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
}
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Sanity: base object must exist
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Build canonical OFR dataset (species filter + clean types + stable IDs)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Steps:
#   (a) keep only I. variabilis
#   (b) enforce Date_and_time POSIXct
#   (c) enforce OFR numeric + keep only finite values
#   (d) define Osculum_ID = Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code
#       (this is the analysis unit for feeding alignment + repeated measures)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_ofr0 <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  filter(Taxa_cod == "I.variabilis") %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Repeat_Code   = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_")),
    OFR           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Date_and_time),
    is.finite(OFR),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Feeding alignment (per Osculum_ID)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Feed_time definition:
#   earliest Date_and_time where Feeding == "after" within each Osculum_ID
#
# Relative time axis:
#   rel_min = minutes since Feed_time (can be negative pre-feeding; we filter later)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_ofr_rel <- df_ofr0 %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code) %>%
  mutate(
    Feed_time = {
      x <- Date_and_time[Feeding == "after"]
      if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) as.POSIXct(NA) else min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    },
    rel_min = as.numeric(difftime(Date_and_time, Feed_time, units = "mins"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(Feed_time), is.finite(rel_min))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Restrict to post-feeding window: 0–12 h
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# rel_h  = continuous hours (used in GAMs)
# rel_30 = 30-min bins (used for visual QA / discretized plots if needed)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_ofr_12h <- df_ofr_rel %>%
  filter(rel_min >= 0, rel_min <= 12 * 60) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_h  = rel_min / 60,
    rel_30 = floor(rel_min / 30) * 30
  )

# OPTIONAL: explicit exclusions (only if truly justified + documented)
# IMPORTANT: these IDs must match the NEW Osculum_ID format (Sponge_RepeatCode)
# df_ofr_12h <- df_ofr_12h %>% filter(!Osculum_ID %in% c("82_A", "83_B"))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Sanity summary (documentation-friendly)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n====================\nDATASET SANITY CHECK\n====================\n")
cat("df_ofr0 rows:     ", nrow(df_ofr0), "\n")
cat("df_ofr_rel rows:  ", nrow(df_ofr_rel), "\n")
cat("df_ofr_12h rows:  ", nrow(df_ofr_12h), "\n")
cat("n sponges:        ", n_distinct(df_ofr_12h$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("n osculum series: ", n_distinct(df_ofr_12h$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")
print(table(df_ofr_12h$Sponge_cod))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) GAM workflow (MATCHING DATA): m0–m4 on df_ofr_12h
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RATIONALE
#   Test time trend after feeding while progressively accounting for hierarchical structure.
#
# MODELS (all via REML)
#   m0: OFR ~ s(rel_h)                                  (population-level smooth)
#   m1: + s(Sponge_cod, bs="re")                        (sponge baseline differences)
#   m2: + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs="fs")                 (sponge-specific time-course deviations)
#   m3: + s(Osculum_ID, bs="re")                        (series-level baseline differences)
#   m4: m3 + sponge-specific time-course deviations     (most flexible of this set)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(mgcv)

df_ofr_12h <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

# m0: population-level smooth
m0_ofr <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 6),
  data   = df_ofr_12h,
  method = "REML"
)

# m1: add sponge random intercept (baseline differences)
m1_ofr <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
  data   = df_ofr_12h,
  method = "REML"
)

# m2: allow sponge-specific deviations over time (factor-smooth interaction)
m2_ofr <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data   = df_ofr_12h,
  method = "REML"
)

# m3: sponge + osculum random intercepts (repeated-measures baseline control)
m3_ofr <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
  data   = df_ofr_12h,
  method = "REML"
)

# m4: osculum random intercept + sponge-specific time-course deviations
m4_ofr <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data   = df_ofr_12h,
  method = "REML"
)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Full documentation output (keep in the knitted report)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n====================\nMODEL SUMMARIES\n====================\n")
summary(m0_ofr); gam.check(m0_ofr)
summary(m1_ofr)
summary(m2_ofr); gam.check(m2_ofr)
summary(m3_ofr); gam.check(m3_ofr)
summary(m4_ofr); gam.check(m4_ofr)

# Model comparisons aligned to Results logic
cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS\n====================\n")
cat("\n--- anova.gam (F tests) ---\n")
print(anova(m0_ofr, m1_ofr, test = "F"))  # baseline sponge differences
print(anova(m0_ofr, m2_ofr, test = "F"))  # sponge-specific time-course (no osculum RE)
print(anova(m3_ofr, m4_ofr, test = "F"))  # sponge-specific time-course after controlling series baseline

cat("\n--- AIC (lower is better; descriptive) ---\n")
print(AIC(m0_ofr, m1_ofr, m2_ofr, m3_ofr, m4_ofr))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Compact key numbers for Results (consistent formatting)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_gam_block <- function(mod) {
  sm <- summary(mod)
  s_tbl <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  s_tbl$term <- rownames(s_tbl); rownames(s_tbl) <- NULL

  if ("p-value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p-value"] <- "p_value"
  if ("p.value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p.value"] <- "p_value"

  list(
    n        = nobs(mod),
    adj_R2   = unname(sm$r.sq),
    dev_expl = 100 * unname(sm$dev.expl),
    s_table  = s_tbl
  )
}

grab_term <- function(block, pattern) {
  st <- block$s_table
  i <- grep(pattern, st$term)
  if (!length(i)) {
    return(data.frame(term = NA, edf = NA, F = NA, p = NA))
  }
  i <- i[1]
  data.frame(
    term = st$term[i],
    edf  = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
    F    = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
    p    = as.numeric(st$p_value[i])
  )
}

B0 <- extract_gam_block(m0_ofr)
B4 <- extract_gam_block(m4_ofr)

m0_time <- grab_term(B0, "s\\(rel_h\\)")
m4_time <- grab_term(B4, "s\\(rel_h\\)")
m4_fs   <- grab_term(B4, "s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)")
m4_re_o <- grab_term(B4, "s\\(Osculum_ID\\)")

cat("\n============================\nOFR feeding 0–12h: KEY NUMBERS\n============================\n")
cat("m0: n=", B0$n,
    " | s(rel_h): edf=", round(m0_time$edf, 2),
    " F=", round(m0_time$F, 2),
    " p=", signif(m0_time$p, 3),
    " | adjR2=", round(B0$adj_R2, 3),
    " | dev%=", round(B0$dev_expl, 2), "\n", sep="")

cat("m4: n=", B4$n,
    " | s(rel_h): edf=", round(m4_time$edf, 2),
    " F=", round(m4_time$F, 2),
    " p=", signif(m4_time$p, 3),
    " | s(rel_h,Sponge_cod): edf=", round(m4_fs$edf, 2),
    " F=", round(m4_fs$F, 2),
    " p=", signif(m4_fs$p, 3),
    " | s(Osculum_ID): edf=", round(m4_re_o$edf, 2),
    " F=", round(m4_re_o$F, 2),
    " p=", signif(m4_re_o$p, 3),
    " | adjR2=", round(B4$adj_R2, 3),
    " | dev%=", round(B4$dev_expl, 2), "\n", sep="")

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Main-text figure: partial effects (A|B) using gratia::draw (robust + consistent styling)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(gratia)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# Panel A: global smooth from m4 (partial effect scale)
pA <- draw(
  m4_ofr,
  select = "s(rel_h)",
  rug = FALSE
) +
  labs(
    title = "A",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = "Partial effect on OFR"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

# Panel B: sponge-specific deviations (colored)
pB <- draw(
  m4_ofr,
  select = "s(rel_h,Sponge_cod)",
  se = FALSE,
  rug = FALSE
) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = cols, name = "Sponge") +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

p_combined <- (pA | pB) + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_OFR_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_0_12h.png"),
  plot     = p_combined,
  width    = 12,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

p_combined

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) QA / sanity checks: alignment + coverage + duplicates
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# (1) Alignment: do we have any points in the 0–30 min bin (rel_30 == 0) per Osculum_ID?
qc_t0 <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points    = n(),
    min_rel_min = min(rel_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_rel_min = max(rel_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_bin0    = any(rel_30 == 0),
    first_time  = min(Date_and_time, na.rm = TRUE),
    last_time   = max(Date_and_time, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(has_bin0, Osculum_ID)

# (2) Coverage: number of distinct 30-min bins per series (max possible = 25 bins: 0..720 by 30)
qc_bins <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code) %>%
  summarise(
    n_bins = n_distinct(rel_30),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(n_bins)

# (3) Duplicates: repeated timestamps and/or multiple points per 30-min bin
qc_dups <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    dup_time = any(duplicated(Date_and_time)),
    dup_bin  = any(duplicated(rel_30)),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n====================\nQA SUMMARY\n====================\n")
cat("\n--- QA: t=0 bin present? (per series) ---\n")
print(table(qc_t0$has_bin0))

cat("\n--- QA: bins per series (0–12h) ---\n")
print(summary(qc_bins$n_bins))

cat("\n--- QA: duplicates ---\n")
print(table(qc_dups$dup_time))
print(table(qc_dups$dup_bin))


####################################################################################################



```


### GAM analysis: OFR response during the first 0–4 hours after feeding (with Osculum_ID random effect)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# GAM analysis: OFR response during the first 0–4 hours after feeding (with Osculum_ID random effect)
# ==================================================================================================
#
# PURPOSE
#   Test whether OFR exhibits a short-term response immediately following feeding,
#   focusing on 0–4 hours post-feeding.
#
# WHY include Osculum_ID?
#   OFR is repeatedly measured within each Osculum_ID series (Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code).
#   Adding s(Osculum_ID, bs="re") accounts for series-level baseline differences and helps
#   test whether any population-level time trend remains after controlling repeated measures.
#
# DATA
#   Uses df_ofr_12h (canonical feeding-aligned dataset; created upstream).
#   Required columns: OFR, rel_h, Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID (and Repeat_Code for reporting).
#
# MODELS (REML)
#   m0_ofr_4h: OFR ~ s(rel_h)                                  (population smooth)
#   m1_ofr_4h: m0 + s(Sponge_cod, bs="re")                     (sponge baseline differences)
#   m2_ofr_4h: m1 + s(Osculum_ID, bs="re")                     (series baseline / repeated measures)
#
# NOTES
#   - Over 0–4 h, k=5 is usually sufficient and avoids overfitting.
#   - The ggplot smooth overlays below are visual-only (do not include random effects).
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Subset: 0–4 h post-feeding (from canonical df_ofr_12h)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("df_ofr_12h"))

df_ofr_4h <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR), !is.na(OFR)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod), !is.na(rel_h)) %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

# Sanity checks: size and structure
cat("\n====================\n0–4 h DATA SANITY\n====================\n")
cat("Number of observations:  ", nrow(df_ofr_4h), "\n")
cat("Osculum_ID series:       ", n_distinct(df_ofr_4h$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("Repeat_Code values:      ", n_distinct(df_ofr_4h$Repeat_Code), "\n")
cat("Sponges:                 ", n_distinct(df_ofr_4h$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")
cat("Observations per sponge:\n")
print(table(df_ofr_4h$Sponge_cod))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) m0: population-level smooth response
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
m0_ofr_4h <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 5),
  data   = df_ofr_4h,
  method = "REML"
)
cat("\n--- m0_ofr_4h: OFR ~ s(rel_h) ---\n")
summary(m0_ofr_4h)
gam.check(m0_ofr_4h)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) m1: add sponge random intercept (baseline differences)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
m1_ofr_4h <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 5) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
  data   = df_ofr_4h,
  method = "REML"
)
cat("\n--- m1_ofr_4h: + s(Sponge_cod, bs='re') ---\n")
summary(m1_ofr_4h)
gam.check(m1_ofr_4h)

cat("\nModel comparison: m0_ofr_4h vs m1_ofr_4h\n")
anova(m0_ofr_4h, m1_ofr_4h, test = "F")

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) m2: add Osculum_ID random intercept (series baseline; repeated measures)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
m2_ofr_4h <- gam(
  OFR ~ s(rel_h, k = 5) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
  data   = df_ofr_4h,
  method = "REML"
)
cat("\n--- m2_ofr_4h: + s(Osculum_ID, bs='re') ---\n")
summary(m2_ofr_4h)
gam.check(m2_ofr_4h)

cat("\nModel comparison: m1_ofr_4h vs m2_ofr_4h\n")
anova(m1_ofr_4h, m2_ofr_4h, test = "F")

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Visualizations (visual-only smooth; random effects not shown)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(df_ofr_4h, aes(rel_h, OFR)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, k = 5), se = TRUE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = expression(OFR~(L~h^{-1})),
    title = "0–4 h post-feeding: raw OFR + population-level GAM smooth (visual only)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(df_ofr_4h, aes(rel_h, OFR)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, k = 5), se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, by = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = expression(OFR~(L~h^{-1})),
    title = "0–4 h post-feeding: raw OFR by sponge (visual-only smooth)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################




```



### Extract GAM results (mgcv): edf / F / p + adj.R2 + deviance explained + n + model comparisons
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

###################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# Extract GAM results (mgcv): edf / F / p + adj.R2 + deviance explained + n + model comparisons
# ==================================================================================================
#
# ASSUMES these objects exist (created upstream in the canonical 0–12 h chunk):
#   - df_ofr_12h
#   - m0_ofr, m1_ofr, m2_ofr, m3_ofr, m4_ofr (and optionally m4_k_ofr if you made one)
#
# And these exist from the 0–4 h chunk above:
#   - df_ofr_4h
#   - m0_ofr_4h, m1_ofr_4h, m2_ofr_4h
#
# OUTPUTS
#   - summary tables for 0–12 h and 0–4 h (one row per model)
#   - anova.gam comparisons (F tests)
#   - CSV export for reproducibility / supplement
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: core extraction from mgcv::summary.gam
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_gam_core <- function(mod, model_name) {
  sm <- summary(mod)

  s_tbl <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  s_tbl$term <- rownames(s_tbl)
  rownames(s_tbl) <- NULL

  # normalize p column naming across mgcv versions
  if ("p-value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p-value"] <- "p_value"
  if ("p.value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p.value"] <- "p_value"

  list(
    model     = model_name,
    n         = nobs(mod),
    adj_r2    = unname(sm$r.sq),
    dev_expl  = unname(sm$dev.expl),  # proportion (0–1)
    s_table   = s_tbl
  )
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: pick terms by pattern (robust to term ordering)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_gam_terms <- function(mod, model_name,
                             term_patterns = c(
                               global_time = "s\\(rel_h\\)",
                               sponge_fs   = "s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)",
                               sponge_re   = "s\\(Sponge_cod\\)",
                               osculum_re  = "s\\(Osculum_ID\\)"
                             )) {

  core <- extract_gam_core(mod, model_name)
  st   <- core$s_table

  grab <- function(pattern) {
    ii <- grep(pattern, st$term)
    if (!length(ii)) return(tibble(edf = NA_real_, F = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
    i <- ii[1]
    tibble(
      edf = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
      F   = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
      p   = as.numeric(if ("p_value" %in% names(st)) st$p_value[i] else NA_real_)
    )
  }

  t_global <- grab(term_patterns["global_time"])
  t_fs     <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_fs"])
  t_sre    <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_re"])
  t_ore    <- grab(term_patterns["osculum_re"])

  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    n = core$n,
    adj_R2 = core$adj_r2,
    dev_expl_pct = 100 * core$dev_expl,

    global_edf = t_global$edf, global_F = t_global$F, global_p = t_global$p,
    fs_edf     = t_fs$edf,     fs_F     = t_fs$F,     fs_p     = t_fs$p,

    spongeRE_edf = t_sre$edf, spongeRE_F = t_sre$F, spongeRE_p = t_sre$p,
    osculumRE_edf = t_ore$edf, osculumRE_F = t_ore$F, osculumRE_p = t_ore$p
  )
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) 0–12 h: build summary table (canonical model names)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("m0_ofr"), exists("m1_ofr"), exists("m2_ofr"), exists("m3_ofr"), exists("m4_ofr"))

gam_12h_list <- list(
  m0_ofr = m0_ofr,
  m1_ofr = m1_ofr,
  m2_ofr = m2_ofr,
  m3_ofr = m3_ofr,
  m4_ofr = m4_ofr
)

# Optional alternative-k model (only if you created it)
if (exists("m4_k_ofr")) gam_12h_list$m4_k_ofr <- m4_k_ofr

summary_12h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_12h_list), function(nm) {
  extract_gam_terms(gam_12h_list[[nm]], model_name = nm)
}))

summary_12h_round <- summary_12h %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

cat("\n====================\nGAM SUMMARY: 0–12 h\n====================\n")
print(summary_12h_round)

# Comparisons you actually use in text
cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS: 0–12 h\n====================\n")
print(anova(m0_ofr, m1_ofr, test = "F"))
print(anova(m0_ofr, m2_ofr, test = "F"))
print(anova(m3_ofr, m4_ofr, test = "F"))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) 0–4 h: summary table + comparisons
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("m0_ofr_4h"), exists("m1_ofr_4h"), exists("m2_ofr_4h"))

gam_4h_list <- list(
  m0_ofr_4h = m0_ofr_4h,
  m1_ofr_4h = m1_ofr_4h,
  m2_ofr_4h = m2_ofr_4h
)

summary_4h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_4h_list), function(nm) {
  extract_gam_terms(gam_4h_list[[nm]], model_name = nm)
}))

summary_4h_round <- summary_4h %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

cat("\n====================\nGAM SUMMARY: 0–4 h\n====================\n")
print(summary_4h_round)

cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS: 0–4 h\n====================\n")
print(anova(m0_ofr_4h, m1_ofr_4h, test = "F"))
print(anova(m1_ofr_4h, m2_ofr_4h, test = "F"))

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Text-ready extract example (choose the model you report)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pick <- summary_12h %>% filter(model == "m4_ofr")

cat("\n=== TEXT-READY (m4_ofr, 0–12 h) ===\n")
cat("n = ", pick$n, "\n", sep = "")
cat("global smooth: edf = ", round(pick$global_edf, 2),
    ", F = ", round(pick$global_F, 2),
    ", p = ", signif(pick$global_p, 3), "\n", sep = "")
cat("sponge-specific deviations (fs): edf = ", round(pick$fs_edf, 2),
    ", F = ", round(pick$fs_F, 2),
    ", p = ", signif(pick$fs_p, 3), "\n", sep = "")
cat("adjusted R2 = ", round(pick$adj_R2, 3),
    "; deviance explained = ", round(pick$dev_expl_pct, 2), "%\n", sep = "")

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Export CSVs (reproducibility)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
if (!exists("out_dir")) out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

write.csv(summary_12h, file.path(out_dir, "GAM_OFR_feeding_0_12h_model_summaries.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(summary_4h,  file.path(out_dir, "GAM_OFR_feeding_0_4h_model_summaries.csv"),  row.names = FALSE)


```

### Paired test (0–30 vs 30–60 min) + per-osculum Spearman correlations (0–12 h and 0–4 h)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# Paired test (0–30 vs 30–60 min) + per-osculum Spearman correlations (0–12 h and 0–4 h)
# Canonical objects:
#   - df_ofr_12h  (built upstream; one source of truth)
# Required columns in df_ofr_12h:
#   - OFR, rel_h, Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID, Repeat_Code (optional for prints)
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------------
# 0) Safety checks + clean subset
# -------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("df_ofr_12h"))
stopifnot(all(c("OFR","rel_h","Sponge_cod","Osculum_ID") %in% names(df_ofr_12h)))

df_ofr_12h2 <- df_ofr_12h %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(OFR), !is.na(OFR),
    is.finite(rel_h), !is.na(rel_h),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("N (df_ofr_12h2):", nrow(df_ofr_12h2), "\n")
cat("Osculum_ID series:", n_distinct(df_ofr_12h2$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("Sponges:", n_distinct(df_ofr_12h2$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Paired test: mean OFR in 0–30 vs 30–60 min (within series)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
paired_df <- df_ofr_12h2 %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h < 1) %>%   # first hour only
  mutate(
    bin = case_when(
      rel_h < 0.5 ~ "t0_0_30min",
      rel_h < 1.0 ~ "t30_30_60min"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, bin) %>%
  summarise(
    OFR_mean = mean(OFR, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_points = n(),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = bin, values_from = OFR_mean) %>%
  filter(!is.na(t0_0_30min), !is.na(t30_30_60min)) %>%
  mutate(delta = t30_30_60min - t0_0_30min)

cat("Paired dataset (0–30 vs 30–60 min):\n")
cat("  Osculum_ID pairs:", nrow(paired_df), "\n\n")

if (nrow(paired_df) >= 1) {
  wilc_res <- wilcox.test(
    paired_df$t0_0_30min,
    paired_df$t30_30_60min,
    paired = TRUE,
    exact  = FALSE
  )
  cat("Wilcoxon paired test (0–30 vs 30–60 min):\n")
  print(wilc_res)
} else {
  cat("Wilcoxon test skipped: no complete pairs found.\n")
}

paired_summary <- paired_df %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs     = n(),
    median_t0   = median(t0_0_30min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_t30  = median(t30_30_60min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_diff   = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_diff     = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nPaired summary:\n")
print(paired_summary)

# Optional paired visualization (each line = Osculum_ID)
ggplot(
  paired_df %>%
    select(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, t0_0_30min, t30_30_60min) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(t0_0_30min, t30_30_60min),
                 names_to = "time_bin", values_to = "OFR_mean") %>%
    mutate(time_bin = factor(time_bin, levels = c("t0_0_30min", "t30_30_60min"))),
  aes(time_bin, OFR_mean, group = Osculum_ID, color = as.character(Sponge_cod))
) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(OFR~(L~h^{-1})),
    title = "Paired comparison (within Osculum_ID): 0–30 min vs 30–60 min post-feeding"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Spearman correlation per Osculum_ID (OFR vs rel_h)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
safe_spearman <- function(x, t, min_n = 4) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(t) & !is.na(x) & !is.na(t)
  x <- x[ok]; t <- t[ok]
  n <- length(x)

  if (n < min_n) return(list(n = n, rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, ok = FALSE, reason = "n<min_n"))
  if (sd(x) == 0) return(list(n = n, rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, ok = FALSE, reason = "sd(OFR)=0"))
  if (sd(t) == 0) return(list(n = n, rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, ok = FALSE, reason = "sd(time)=0"))

  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, t, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  list(n = n, rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, ok = TRUE, reason = NA_character_)
}

spearman_by_osculum <- function(df, window_label, min_n = 4) {
  res <- df %>%
    group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod) %>%
    summarise(
      tmp = list(safe_spearman(OFR, rel_h, min_n = min_n)),
      n   = tmp[[1]]$n,
      rho = tmp[[1]]$rho,
      p   = tmp[[1]]$p,
      ok  = tmp[[1]]$ok,
      reason = tmp[[1]]$reason,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      window = window_label,
      p_fdr = ifelse(is.na(p), NA_real_, p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))
    ) %>%
    select(-tmp)

  cat("\nSpearman diagnostics for window:", window_label, "\n")
  cat("  Total series:", nrow(res), "\n")
  cat("  Valid tests:", sum(res$ok), "\n")
  cat("  Excluded:", sum(!res$ok), "\n")
  if (sum(!res$ok) > 0) {
    print(table(res$reason, useNA = "ifany"))
  }
  res
}

# a) 0–12 h
sp_12h <- spearman_by_osculum(df_ofr_12h2 %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 12), "0-12h", min_n = 4)

# b) 0–4 h
sp_4h  <- spearman_by_osculum(df_ofr_12h2 %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4),  "0-4h",  min_n = 4)

sp_all <- bind_rows(sp_12h, sp_4h)

cat("\nSpearman per Osculum_ID summary:\n")
sp_all %>%
  group_by(window) %>%
  summarise(
    n_series    = n(),
    n_valid     = sum(ok),
    n_sig_p05   = sum(p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig_fdr05 = sum(p_fdr < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rho  = median(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rho    = mean(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()

top_abs_rho <- sp_all %>%
  filter(ok) %>%
  group_by(window) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(rho))) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("\nTop |rho| (per window):\n")
print(top_abs_rho)

# Plots
ggplot(sp_all %>% filter(ok), aes(rho)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Spearman rho (OFR vs time since feeding)",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of per-osculum Spearman correlations"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(sp_all %>% filter(ok), aes(Sponge_cod, rho, color = as.character(Sponge_cod))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Sponge",
    y = "Spearman rho",
    title = "Per-osculum rho values by sponge"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Optional exports (use out_dir if defined)
# write.csv(paired_df, file.path(out_dir, "OFR_paired_0_30_vs_30_60min_by_OsculumID.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(sp_all,    file.path(out_dir, "OFR_spearman_by_OsculumID_0_12h_and_0_4h.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################



```



### Synchrony in pumping rate (OFR) between paired sponges: Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot) 
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

################################################################################
# Synchrony in pumping rate (OFR) between paired sponges
# Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot)
#
# INPUT:
#   df_base must include: Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Sponge_cod, OFR
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   1) df_pairwide_ofr       : paired-wide table (A/B) per timestamp and run
#   2) cor_pair_by_run_ofr   : Pearson & Spearman per Pair_ID × Repeat_Index
#   3) p_ofr_box             : boxplot of Spearman rho distributions per Pair_ID
#   4) summary_OFR           : mean/median rho per pair + Overall
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# USER SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
min_nOFR <- 9  # minimum paired timestamps per Pair_ID × Repeat_Index to compute correlation

pair_colors <- c(
  "82-83" = "#FF69B4",
  "84-85" = "#7A378B",
  "86-87" = "#104E8B"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1 — Clean + keep only finite OFR rows (minimal types)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_ofr <- df_base %>%
  mutate(
    Repeat_Index  = as.integer(Repeat_Index),
    Sponge_cod    = as.character(Sponge_cod),
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    OFR           = as.numeric(OFR)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2 — Keep only columns needed for pairing
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_keep <- df_ofr %>%
  select(Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Sponge_cod, OFR) %>%
  arrange(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Sponge_cod)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3 — Keep only true parallel measurements (exactly two sponges)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_keep2 <- df_keep %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Sponge_cod) == 2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Quick sanity check: should only have 2 sponges per timepoint
sanity_nsp <- df_keep2 %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  summarise(n_sponges = n_distinct(Sponge_cod), .groups = "drop") %>%
  count(n_sponges)

print(sanity_nsp)
stopifnot(all(sanity_nsp$n_sponges == 2))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4 — Build paired-wide table (A/B)
#   A = smaller Sponge_cod, B = larger Sponge_cod (stable biological definition)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_pairwide_ofr <- df_keep2 %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  summarise(
    A_id    = min(Sponge_cod),
    B_id    = max(Sponge_cod),
    Pair_ID = paste(A_id, B_id, sep = "-"),
    A       = OFR[Sponge_cod == A_id][1],
    B       = OFR[Sponge_cod == B_id][1],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  select(Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Pair_ID, A, B) %>%
  arrange(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Pair_ID)

# QC: ensure no missing A/B
stopifnot(all(is.finite(df_pairwide_ofr$A)), all(is.finite(df_pairwide_ofr$B)))

# QC: pairs per run (expected 3 if design fixed)
pairs_per_run <- df_pairwide_ofr %>%
  distinct(Repeat_Index, Pair_ID) %>%
  count(Repeat_Index, name = "n_pairs")
print(pairs_per_run)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5 — Correlation WITHIN each run (Pair_ID × Repeat_Index)
#   This yields a distribution of correlation values per pair.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cor_pair_by_run_ofr <- df_pairwide_ofr %>%
  group_by(Pair_ID, Repeat_Index) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points   = n(),
    pearson_r  = if (n_points >= min_nOFR) cor(A, B, method = "pearson",  use = "complete.obs") else NA_real_,
    spearman_r = if (n_points >= min_nOFR) cor(A, B, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs") else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(spearman_r))  # Spearman is what we plot; keep those defined

# Quick check: number of runs contributing per pair
runs_per_pair <- cor_pair_by_run_ofr %>%
  count(Pair_ID, name = "n_runs_with_corr")
print(runs_per_pair)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 6 — Summary table: per pair + Overall
#   mean / median Spearman rho + 95% bootstrap CI half-width (±CI)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

bootstrap_ci_halfwidth <- function(x, conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 3) return(NA_real_)

  boot_means <- replicate(nboot, mean(sample(x, replace = TRUE)))

  alpha <- (1 - conf) / 2
  ci <- quantile(boot_means, probs = c(alpha, 1 - alpha), names = FALSE)

  (ci[2] - ci[1]) / 2
}

summarise_cor_table <- function(cor_by_run, r_col = "spearman_r",
                                conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {

  stopifnot(all(c("Pair_ID", "Repeat_Index", r_col) %in% names(cor_by_run)))

  per_pair <- cor_by_run %>%
    group_by(Pair_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(Pair_ID)

  overall <- cor_by_run %>%
    summarise(
      Pair_ID  = "Overall",
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot)
    )

  bind_rows(per_pair, overall)
}

summary_OFR <- summarise_cor_table(
  cor_pair_by_run_ofr,
  r_col = "spearman_r"
)

summary_OFR

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 7 — Publication-style boxplot (white background, no grid)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_ofr_box <- ggplot(cor_pair_by_run_ofr, aes(x = Pair_ID, y = spearman_r, fill = Pair_ID)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    width = 0.6,
    outlier.shape = 21,
    outlier.size = 2,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.12,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pair_colors) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 15) +
  labs(
    title    = "Within-run synchrony in pumping rate (OFR)",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "Sponge pair",
    y = expression("Spearman correlation (" * rho * ")")
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13)
  )

p_ofr_box

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 8 — Save figure
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Spearman_boxplot_pairs_OFR.png",
  plot     = p_ofr_box,
  path     = "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R",
  width    = 7,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white"
)

################################################################################
# OPTIONAL — If you still want overlay histograms (Pearson / Spearman)
#   These are based on cor_pair_by_run_ofr.
################################################################################

# Pearson (optional)
p_pearson <- ggplot(cor_pair_by_run_ofr, aes(x = pearson_r, fill = Pair_ID)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, position = "identity", alpha = 0.45, color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pair_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Within-run synchrony in OFR: Pearson correlations",
    subtitle = "Each value is Pearson r computed within a run (Repeat_Index)",
    x = "Pearson correlation r (A vs B)",
    y = "Number of runs",
    fill = "Pair_ID"
  )

# Spearman (optional)
p_spearman_hist <- ggplot(cor_pair_by_run_ofr, aes(x = spearman_r, fill = Pair_ID)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 12, position = "identity", alpha = 0.45, color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pair_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Within-run synchrony in OFR: Spearman correlations",
    subtitle = "Each value is Spearman rho computed within a run (Repeat_Index)",
    x = expression("Spearman correlation (" * rho * ")"),
    y = "Number of runs",
    fill = "Pair_ID"
  )

# Print if needed:
# p_pearson
# p_spearman_hist


```


## Oxygen removal (ΔO₂)

### Diel (day–night / time-of-day) analysis for ΔO₂ using GAMs with AR(1)

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# Diel (day–night / time-of-day) analysis for ΔO₂ using GAMs with AR(1)
# Canonical input: iv_feeding_base  (from import chunk)
# Canonical series unit: Osculum_ID = Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code
# ==================================================================================================
#
# PURPOSE
#   Test whether oxygen removal (ΔO₂) shows a diel pattern in the lab:
#     (A) smooth cyclic effect across 24 h (tod)
#     (B) simple Day vs Night difference
#     (C) allow sponge-specific diel deviations (heterogeneous patterns)
#
# KEY NOTES
#   - AR(1) accounts for temporal autocorrelation within each Osculum_ID.
#   - rel_sec is defined within Osculum_ID as seconds since first observation in that series.
#   - DayNight definition: Day = 06:00–18:00 (06:00 inclusive, 18:00 exclusive).
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)

has_gratia    <- requireNamespace("gratia", quietly = TRUE)
has_patchwork <- requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixed palette (chapter-wide)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cols <- c(
  "82" = "#FF69B4",
  "83" = "#8B3A62",
  "84" = "#E066FF",
  "85" = "#7A378B",
  "86" = "#1C86EE",
  "87" = "#104E8B"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Hard input checks
# ------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))

REQ <- c("Sponge_cod", "Repeat_Code", "Date_and_time", "ΔO2")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("iv_feeding_base missing: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Build df_do2 (self-contained)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
df_do2 <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Repeat_Code   = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_")),
    dO2           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Date_and_time), is.finite(dO2), !is.na(Osculum_ID)) %>%
  mutate(
    # time-of-day (0–24, continuous)
    tod = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")) +
          as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%M"))/60 +
          as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%S"))/3600,
    hour = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")),
    DayNight = ifelse(hour >= 6 & hour < 18, "Day", "Night"),
    DayNight = factor(DayNight, levels = c("Day","Night"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_sec = as.numeric(difftime(Date_and_time, min(Date_and_time, na.rm = TRUE), units = "secs"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(is.finite(tod), is.finite(rel_sec)) %>%
  droplevels()

cat("ΔO₂ diel dataset (df_do2):\n")
cat("  N rows:", nrow(df_do2), "\n")
cat("  Sponges:", n_distinct(df_do2$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  Oscula :", n_distinct(df_do2$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("  tod range:", paste(range(df_do2$tod, na.rm = TRUE), collapse = " – "), "\n\n")

print(table(df_do2$Sponge_cod))
print(table(df_do2$DayNight))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Models
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# A) population-level cyclic smooth
m_do2_tod <- gam(
  dO2 ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8),
  data        = df_do2,
  method      = "REML",
  knots       = list(tod = c(0, 24)),
  correlation = corAR1(form = ~ rel_sec | Osculum_ID)
)

cat("\n--- Model A: ΔO₂ ~ s(tod, cc) + AR(1) ---\n")
print(summary(m_do2_tod))
cat("\nGAM check (Model A):\n")
print(gam.check(m_do2_tod))

# B) Day vs Night
m_do2_dn <- gam(
  dO2 ~ DayNight,
  data        = df_do2,
  method      = "REML",
  correlation = corAR1(form = ~ rel_sec | Osculum_ID)
)

cat("\n--- Model B: ΔO₂ ~ DayNight + AR(1) ---\n")
print(summary(m_do2_dn))

cat("\nAIC comparison (Model A vs Model B):\n")
print(AIC(m_do2_tod, m_do2_dn))

# C) sponge-specific deviations around population diel curve
m_do2_sponge <- gam(
  dO2 ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8) + s(tod, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data        = df_do2,
  method      = "REML",
  knots       = list(tod = c(0, 24)),
  correlation = corAR1(form = ~ rel_sec | Osculum_ID)
)

cat("\n--- Model C: ΔO₂ ~ s(tod, cc) + s(tod, Sponge_cod, fs) + AR(1) ---\n")
print(summary(m_do2_sponge))
cat("\nGAM check (Model C):\n")
print(gam.check(m_do2_sponge))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Base mgcv plots (quick diagnostic, matches your structure)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
op <- par(no.readonly = TRUE)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4.5, 5.2, 3.2, 1.2), mgp = c(2.6, 0.8, 0), las = 1, bty = "l")

plot(
  m_do2_tod,
  select = 1,
  shade = TRUE,
  shade.col = "grey80",
  rug = TRUE,
  xlab = "Time of day (h)",
  ylab = expression("Partial effect on "*Delta*O[2]),
  main = "A  Population-level diel smooth (ΔO₂)"
)
axis(1, at = seq(0, 24, by = 6))

plot(
  m_do2_sponge,
  select = 2,
  pages  = 1,
  se     = FALSE,
  rug    = FALSE,
  xlab   = "Time of day (h)",
  ylab   = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)",
  main   = "B  Sponge-specific diel deviations"
)
axis(1, at = seq(0, 24, by = 6))

par(op)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Optional: publication-style (gratia + patchwork), same look as chapter
# ------------------------------------------------------------
if (has_patchwork) {
  library(patchwork)

  p_combined <- (pA_do2 + pB_do2) +
    plot_layout(ncol = 2, guides = "collect")

  p_combined <- p_combined + plot_annotation(theme = theme(
    legend.position = "right"
  ))

  print(p_combined)

  if (!exists("out_dir")) out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
  dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_diel_GAM_population_and_sponge.png"),
    plot     = p_combined,
    width    = 12,
    height   = 5,
    dpi      = 300
  )
} else {
  print(pA_do2)
  print(pB_do2)
}

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################



```

### Build canonical ΔO2 (oxygen removal) feeding-aligned datasets
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# Build canonical ΔO2 (oxygen removal) feeding-aligned datasets
# One source of truth: iv_feeding_base
#
# OUTPUT OBJECTS (used downstream):
#   - df_do2_0    : filtered, cleaned base data (I. variabilis only; finite ΔO2)
#   - df_do2_rel  : same data aligned to feeding time (rel_sec = seconds since feeding)
#   - df_do2_12h  : post-feeding window 0–12 h + AR_start markers (for bam AR(1))
#   - df_do2_4h   : post-feeding window 0–4 h  + AR_start markers (for bam AR(1))
#
# KEY DEFINITIONS:
#   - Osculum_ID  = Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code  (identical convention to the OFR workflow)
#   - Feed_time   = earliest timestamp where Feeding == "after" (per Osculum_ID)
#   - rel_sec     = seconds since Feed_time (per row)
#   - rel_h       = rel_sec / 3600 (hours since feeding)
#   - AR_start    = TRUE only for the first row of each Osculum_ID series (required by bam(rho=..., AR.start=...))
#
# NOTES:
#   - This chunk is intentionally self-contained and should be run once per knit.
#   - Any rows/series with missing feeding alignment (no "after") are excluded.
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# ================================================================================================
# 1) Canonical settings
```
