```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
# ================================================================================================
gap_warn_sec   <- 5 * 60      # not used in this chunk (used in QC chunk), kept here for consistency
window_12h_sec <- 12 * 3600
window_4h_sec  <- 4  * 3600

# ================================================================================================
# 2) Input checks (single source of truth)
# ================================================================================================
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))

REQ <- c("Taxa_cod","Date_and_time","Sponge_cod","Repeat_Code","Feeding","ΔO2")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss) > 0) {
  stop("iv_feeding_base is missing required columns: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))
}

# ================================================================================================
# 3) Base filter + canonical identifiers
#    - Species filter (I. variabilis)
#    - Coerce types (Date_and_time POSIXct, Repeat_Code character)
#    - Construct Osculum_ID consistently with OFR: Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code
#    - Coerce ΔO2 to numeric (stored as dO2) and keep finite values only
# ================================================================================================
df_do2_0 <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  filter(Taxa_cod == "I.variabilis") %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Repeat_Code   = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_")),
    dO2           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Date_and_time),
    is.finite(dO2),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, Date_and_time)

cat("Base ΔO2 dataset (df_do2_0)\n")
cat("  rows:   ", nrow(df_do2_0), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df_do2_0$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("  sponges:", n_distinct(df_do2_0$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================================
# 4) Feeding alignment (per Osculum_ID series)
#    Feed_time = earliest timestamp where Feeding == "after"
#    rel_sec   = seconds since Feed_time
#
#    Series with no Feeding == "after" are not alignable -> excluded.
# ================================================================================================
df_do2_rel <- df_do2_0 %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod) %>%
  mutate(
    Feed_time = {
      x <- Date_and_time[Feeding == "after"]
      if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) as.POSIXct(NA) else min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    },
    rel_sec = as.numeric(difftime(Date_and_time, Feed_time, units = "secs"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(Feed_time), is.finite(rel_sec))

cat("Aligned dataset (df_do2_rel)\n")
cat("  rows:   ", nrow(df_do2_rel), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df_do2_rel$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================================
# 5) Post-feeding windows + AR_start markers
#    AR_start is required for bam(..., rho=..., AR.start=...) and must be TRUE exactly once per series.
# ================================================================================================
df_do2_12h <- df_do2_rel %>%
  filter(rel_sec >= 0, rel_sec <= window_12h_sec) %>%
  mutate(rel_h = rel_sec / 3600) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_sec) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  mutate(AR_start = row_number() == 1) %>%
  ungroup()

df_do2_4h <- df_do2_12h %>%
  filter(rel_sec <= window_4h_sec) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_sec) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  mutate(AR_start = row_number() == 1) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("Windows:\n")
cat("  df_do2_12h rows:", nrow(df_do2_12h),
    "| oscula:", n_distinct(df_do2_12h$Osculum_ID),
    "| sponges:", n_distinct(df_do2_12h$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  df_do2_4h  rows:", nrow(df_do2_4h),
    "| oscula:", n_distinct(df_do2_4h$Osculum_ID),
    "| sponges:", n_distinct(df_do2_4h$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================================
# 6) Sanity checks
#    - Every Osculum_ID that exists in the window must have >= 1 row
#    - AR_start must be TRUE exactly once per Osculum_ID (per window)
# ================================================================================================
stopifnot(all(df_do2_12h %>% count(Osculum_ID) %>% pull(n) >= 1))

ar_chk_12h <- df_do2_12h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(n_true = sum(AR_start), .groups = "drop")

ar_chk_4h <- df_do2_4h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(n_true = sum(AR_start), .groups = "drop")

stopifnot(all(ar_chk_12h$n_true == 1))
stopifnot(all(ar_chk_4h$n_true  == 1))

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

```


### ΔO2 aligned time series QC + facet plot (0–12 h)

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# ΔO2 aligned time series QC + facet plot (0–12 h)
# ==================================================================================================
#
# PURPOSE
#   1) Summarize basic time-series QC per Osculum_ID (sampling interval, gaps, duplicates, monotonicity)
#   2) Visualize raw aligned ΔO2 trajectories during the 0–12 h post-feeding window
#
# INPUTS (created upstream):
#   - df_do2_rel  : aligned dataset with columns:
#       Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Date_and_time, Feed_time, rel_sec, dO2
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   - qc_do2            : per-series QC summary table
#   - p_do2_facets_12h  : ggplot (faceted by Osculum_ID)
#
# NOTES
#   - This chunk is descriptive QC only (no exclusions are applied here).
#   - "gaps" are defined relative to the empirically observed sampling interval in your data.
#   - If you want QC-based exclusion, do it explicitly in a separate chunk (so decisions are traceable).
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ================================================================================================
# 0) Preconditions + canonical settings
# ================================================================================================
stopifnot(exists("df_do2_rel"))

gap_warn_sec   <- 5 * 60      # flag time gaps > 5 minutes within a series
window_12h_sec <- 12 * 3600   # plotting/QC window: 0–12 hours post-feeding

# ================================================================================================
# 1) QC table per Osculum_ID series
#    - dt diagnostics from rel_sec diffs (median/mean/sd)
#    - duplicates in rel_sec (often indicate duplicate timestamps after alignment)
#    - non-monotonic rel_sec (should not happen; indicates ordering/alignment issues)
#    - gap counts and max gap
# ================================================================================================
qc_do2 <- df_do2_rel %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_sec) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod) %>%
  summarise(
    # series extent
    n_obs      = n(),
    t_min_sec  = min(rel_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    t_max_sec  = max(rel_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    duration_h = (t_max_sec - t_min_sec) / 3600,

    # sampling interval diagnostics
    dt_sec      = list(diff(rel_sec)),
    n_dt        = length(dt_sec[[1]]),
    dt_median_s = if (n_dt > 0) median(dt_sec[[1]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    dt_mean_s   = if (n_dt > 0) mean(dt_sec[[1]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    dt_sd_s     = if (n_dt > 1) sd(dt_sec[[1]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,

    # duplicates / monotonicity checks
    n_dup_time = sum(duplicated(rel_sec)),
    n_nonmono  = sum(dt_sec[[1]] < 0, na.rm = TRUE),

    # gaps (suspicious large jumps in time)
    n_gaps    = sum(dt_sec[[1]] > gap_warn_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_gap_s = if (n_dt > 0) max(dt_sec[[1]], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,

    # basic coverage check
    has_t0 = any(rel_sec == 0, na.rm = TRUE),

    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    flag_nonmono = n_nonmono > 0,
    flag_gappy   = n_gaps > 0
  )

cat("QC top 10 (worst gaps):\n")
print(qc_do2 %>% arrange(desc(flag_gappy), desc(max_gap_s)) %>% head(10))

cat("\nTypical sampling interval (median across series): ",
    median(qc_do2$dt_median_s, na.rm = TRUE), " sec\n\n", sep = "")

# ================================================================================================
# 2) Facet plot: raw aligned ΔO2 (0–12 h), one panel per Osculum_ID
#    - uses chapter-wide sponge palette (cols) if already defined
# ================================================================================================
df_plot <- df_do2_rel %>%
  filter(rel_sec >= 0, rel_sec <= window_12h_sec) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_h       = rel_sec / 3600,
    Osculum_lab = gsub("^([0-9]+)_", "\\1.", as.character(Osculum_ID))  # cosmetic: 84_A -> 84.A
  )

# Palette (only define if not already defined globally)
if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

p_do2_facets_12h <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(rel_h, dO2, color = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.75, size = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Osculum_lab, scales = "free_y", ncol = 6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 12, 2),
    limits = c(0, 12),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 12, 2), " h")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Time since feeding",
    y = expression(Delta*O[2]~(mu*mol~O[2]~L^{-1}))
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.6),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.spacing    = unit(0.6, "lines")
  )

p_do2_facets_12h

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

```


###  ΔO2 (oxygen removal) — aligned 0–12 h models with AR(1) autocorrelation + partial-effects figure

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# ΔO2 (oxygen removal) — aligned 0–12 h models with AR(1) autocorrelation + partial-effects figure
# ==================================================================================================
#
# PURPOSE
#   Fit a set of candidate GAMMs (mgcv::bam) to aligned ΔO2 time series (0–12 h post-feeding),
#   explicitly accounting for temporal autocorrelation via an AR(1) structure.
#
# WHY bam + AR(1)?
#   The aligned ΔO2 series are repeated measurements within each Osculum_ID and are typically
#   autocorrelated. In mgcv::bam, AR(1) is handled via:
#      rho      = lag-1 correlation parameter (initial guess or estimated later)
#      AR.start = logical vector marking the first observation of each series
#
# INPUTS (created upstream):
#   - df_do2_12h : aligned post-feeding dataset with:
#       dO2 (numeric response), rel_h (hours since feeding),
#       Sponge_cod (factor), Osculum_ID (factor), AR_start (TRUE once per series)
#
# MODELS (fREML + discrete):
#   m0_do2 : dO2 ~ s(rel_h)                                  (population time trend)
#   m1_do2 : m0_do2 + s(Sponge_cod, bs="re")                 (sponge baseline differences)
#   m2_do2 : m0_do2 + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs="fs")          (sponge-specific time deviations; shrinkage)
#   m3_do2 : m1_do2 + s(Osculum_ID, bs="re")                 (osculum/series baseline differences)
#   m4_do2 : s(rel_h) + s(Osculum_ID,re) + fs(rel_h,Sponge)  (series baseline + sponge-specific deviations)
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   - anova() comparisons (F tests; nested models)
#   - models_do2_summary table (n, adj.R2, deviance explained, fREML)
#   - two-panel partial-effects plot from m4_do2:
#       A) global smooth s(rel_h)
#       B) sponge-specific deviation fs(rel_h, Sponge_cod)
#
# NOTES
#   - rho0 is an initial guess; you later estimate rho from residuals and refit consistently.
#   - In the partial-effects plot, we EXCLUDE s(Osculum_ID) to show population-level curves
#     (i.e., no series-specific intercept shifts).
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ================================================================================================
# 0) Preconditions
# ================================================================================================
stopifnot(exists("df_do2_12h"))
stopifnot(all(c("rel_h", "AR_start", "Osculum_ID", "Sponge_cod", "dO2") %in% names(df_do2_12h)))

# Palette (chapter-wide; define only if missing)
if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# Initial AR(1) guess (updated later via residual-based estimate)
rho0 <- 0.6

# ================================================================================================
# 1) Fit candidate models (0–12 h; AR1 via rho + AR.start)
# ================================================================================================
m0_do2 <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho0,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

m1_do2 <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho0,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

m2_do2 <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho0,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

m3_do2 <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho0,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

m4_do2 <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho0,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

# ================================================================================================
# 2) Model comparisons (nested; F-tests)
# ================================================================================================
cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS (0–12 h)\n====================\n")
print(anova(m0_do2, m1_do2, test = "F"))  # add sponge RE
print(anova(m1_do2, m2_do2, test = "F"))  # allow sponge-specific time deviations
print(anova(m1_do2, m3_do2, test = "F"))  # add osculum RE
print(anova(m3_do2, m4_do2, test = "F"))  # add sponge-specific deviations on top of osculum baseline

# ================================================================================================
# 3) Compact model table for reporting (fit metrics)
# ================================================================================================
model_row <- function(m, name) {
  s <- summary(m)
  tibble(
    model    = name,
    n        = nobs(m),
    adj_R2   = unname(s$r.sq),
    dev_expl = unname(s$dev.expl),  # proportion (0–1)
    fREML    = m$gcv.ubre
  )
}

models_do2_summary <- bind_rows(
  model_row(m0_do2, "m0: s(rel_h)"),
  model_row(m1_do2, "m1: + Sponge RE"),
  model_row(m2_do2, "m2: + fs(rel_h, Sponge)"),
  model_row(m3_do2, "m3: + Sponge RE + Osculum RE"),
  model_row(m4_do2, "m4: + Osculum RE + fs(rel_h, Sponge)")
)

cat("\n====================\nMODEL FIT SUMMARY (0–12 h)\n====================\n")
print(models_do2_summary)

# ================================================================================================
# 4) Partial-effects figure from m4_do2
#    - exclude s(Osculum_ID) so curves reflect population-level patterns (no series intercept shifts)
# ================================================================================================
grid <- expand.grid(
  rel_h      = seq(0, 12, length.out = 250),
  Sponge_cod = levels(df_do2_12h$Sponge_cod),
  Osculum_ID = levels(df_do2_12h$Osculum_ID)[1]  # placeholder; excluded in prediction
)

pr <- predict(
  m4_do2,
  newdata = grid,
  type    = "terms",
  se.fit  = TRUE,
  exclude = "s(Osculum_ID)"
)

term_names <- colnames(pr$fit)
term_global <- term_names[grepl("^s\\(rel_h\\)$", term_names)]
term_fs     <- term_names[grepl("s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)", term_names)]

stopifnot(length(term_global) == 1)
stopifnot(length(term_fs) == 1)

# Panel A: global smooth with 95% CI
dfA <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    term = pr$fit[, term_global],
    se   = pr$se.fit[, term_global]
  ) %>%
  filter(Sponge_cod == levels(df_do2_12h$Sponge_cod)[1]) %>%
  mutate(
    lo = term - 1.96 * se,
    hi = term + 1.96 * se
  )

pA <- ggplot(dfA, aes(rel_h, term)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "A",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = expression("Partial effect on "~Delta*O[2])
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Panel B: sponge-specific deviations (fs term)
dfB <- grid %>% mutate(dev = pr$fit[, term_fs])

pB <- ggplot(dfB, aes(rel_h, dev, color = Sponge_cod, group = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)",
    color = "Sponge"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

(pA + pB) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.15))

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

```

### OPTIONAL — Estimate AR(1) rho from residual lag-1 correlation + refit the final model

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# OPTIONAL — Estimate AR(1) rho from residual lag-1 correlation + refit the final model
# ==================================================================================================
#
# Why this chunk exists?
#   In mgcv::bam, AR(1) autocorrelation is controlled by the parameter rho (lag-1 correlation).
#   Using a fixed guess (e.g., rho0 = 0.6) is fine for exploration, but for final reporting it is
#   better to estimate rho from the residuals and refit with that value.
#
# What this does:
#   1) Compute residuals from the current model (m4_do2)
#   2) Compute per-series lag-1 correlation of residuals (within each Osculum_ID)
#   3) Use the median lag-1 correlation as rho_hat (clipped to [0, 0.95])
#   4) Refit the same model with rho = rho_hat
#
# Inputs required:
#   - df_do2_12h with: dO2, rel_h, Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, AR_start
#   - m4_do2 (already fitted, typically using rho0)
#
# Output:
#   - m4_do2_final (refit model with rho_hat)
#   - Diagnostic plot: lag-1 residual correlation before vs after
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("df_do2_12h"), exists("m4_do2"))
stopifnot(all(c("dO2","rel_h","Osculum_ID","Sponge_cod","AR_start") %in% names(df_do2_12h)))

# -----------------------------
# Helper: lag-1 correlation
# -----------------------------
lag1_cor <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 3) return(NA_real_)
  cor(x[-length(x)], x[-1])
}

# -----------------------------
# Estimate rho from residuals
# IMPORTANT: ordering must reflect time within each Osculum_ID.
# Here we order by rel_h (works because rel_h is monotonic by construction post-alignment).
# If you kept rel_sec, you can replace rel_h with rel_sec.
# -----------------------------
estimate_rho_from_model <- function(model, df, resid_type = "response") {

  df_res <- df %>%
    arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_h) %>%
    mutate(resid = residuals(model, type = resid_type))

  rho_by <- df_res %>%
    group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      rho_lag1 = lag1_cor(resid),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  rho_hat <- median(rho_by$rho_lag1, na.rm = TRUE)
  rho_hat <- max(min(rho_hat, 0.95), 0)  # keep within stable bounds

  list(rho_hat = rho_hat, rho_by = rho_by)
}

# -----------------------------
# 1) Estimate rho from current model (typically rho0)
# -----------------------------
rho_before <- estimate_rho_from_model(m4_do2, df_do2_12h)
rho_hat <- rho_before$rho_hat

cat("\nEstimated rho_hat (median lag-1 residual cor) =", round(rho_hat, 3), "\n")
print(summary(rho_before$rho_by$rho_lag1))

# -----------------------------
# 2) Refit m4 with rho_hat (do NOT overwrite by default)
# -----------------------------
m4_do2_final <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data     = df_do2_12h,
  method   = "fREML",
  discrete = TRUE,
  rho      = rho_hat,
  AR.start = df_do2_12h$AR_start
)

# -----------------------------
# 3) Re-estimate rho after refit (QC)
# -----------------------------
rho_after <- estimate_rho_from_model(m4_do2_final, df_do2_12h)

cat("rho_hat after refit (QC) =", round(rho_after$rho_hat, 3), "\n")

rho_cmp <- bind_rows(
  rho_before$rho_by %>% mutate(stage = "before"),
  rho_after$rho_by  %>% mutate(stage = "after")
)

ggplot(rho_cmp, aes(stage, rho_lag1)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Lag-1 residual correlation (per Osculum_ID)",
    title = "Residual lag-1 correlation per Osculum_ID (before vs after refit)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################


```

### ΔO2 models for 0–4 h post-feeding (bam + AR(1)) + comparisons + reporting tables + figures
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# ΔO2 models for 0–4 h post-feeding (bam + AR(1)) + comparisons + reporting tables + figures
# ==================================================================================================
#
# PURPOSE
#   Fit ΔO2 (dO2) time-since-feeding models in the early window (0–4 h),
#   accounting for within-series temporal autocorrelation via AR(1) in mgcv::bam.
#
# ASSUMES (from your canonical preprocessing chunks)
#   - df_do2_4h exists and already includes:
#       dO2, rel_h, rel_sec (optional), Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, AR_start
#   - Osculum_ID is osculum-level series ID (same construction as OFR: Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code)
#
# MODELS (structure mirrors 0–12h logic, just shortened window)
#   m0_do2_4h: dO2 ~ s(rel_h)
#   m1_do2_4h: + s(Sponge_cod, bs="re")                     (sponge baseline differences)
#   m2_do2_4h: + s(Osculum_ID, bs="re")                     (series baseline differences)
#   m3_do2_4h: + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs="fs")              (sponge-specific time deviations)
#
# NOTES
#   - In 0–4h signal may be weak; avoid over-interpreting wiggles.
#   - P-values for bs="re" are not the main inference; use model comparisons.
#   - For fair comparison of models, use the SAME rho for all models.
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Input checks
# ---------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("df_do2_4h"))
stopifnot(all(c("dO2","rel_h","AR_start","Osculum_ID","Sponge_cod") %in% names(df_do2_4h)))

# Optional: keep rel_sec if exists (used only for rho estimation ordering)
has_rel_sec <- "rel_sec" %in% names(df_do2_4h)

# Palette (only for plotting)
if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c("82"="#FF69B4","83"="#8B3A62","84"="#E066FF","85"="#7A378B","86"="#1C86EE","87"="#104E8B")
}

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Choose rho to use (canonical decision)
#    Option A (recommended): reuse rho from the 0–12h final model if you already estimated it.
#    Option B: estimate rho within 0–4h (optional; can be noisy due to shorter window).
# ---------------------------------------------------------
rho0 <- 0.6

# If you already have a good rho from 0–12h (e.g., rho_hat ~ 0.89), set it here:
rho_use <- NA_real_
if (exists("rho_hat") && is.finite(rho_hat)) rho_use <- rho_hat     # if you stored it
if (exists("rho_do2") && is.finite(rho_do2)) rho_use <- rho_do2     # alternative name you used earlier

if (!is.finite(rho_use)) {
  rho_use <- rho0
  cat("rho_use not found from 0–12h -> using rho0 =", rho_use, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Using rho from 0–12h (rho_use) =", round(rho_use, 3), "\n")
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Fit candidate models (ALL with same rho_use)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
m0_do2_4h <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6),
  data = df_do2_4h, method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE,
  rho = rho_use, AR.start = df_do2_4h$AR_start
)

m1_do2_4h <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
  data = df_do2_4h, method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE,
  rho = rho_use, AR.start = df_do2_4h$AR_start
)

m2_do2_4h <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
  data = df_do2_4h, method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE,
  rho = rho_use, AR.start = df_do2_4h$AR_start
)

m3_do2_4h <- bam(
  dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data = df_do2_4h, method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE,
  rho = rho_use, AR.start = df_do2_4h$AR_start
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Model comparisons (structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- Model comparisons (0–4h; SAME rho) ---\n")
print(anova(m0_do2_4h, m1_do2_4h, test = "F"))  # sponge baseline
print(anova(m1_do2_4h, m2_do2_4h, test = "F"))  # osculum baseline
print(anova(m2_do2_4h, m3_do2_4h, test = "F"))  # add sponge-specific time deviations

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Report-ready model fit table
# ---------------------------------------------------------
model_row <- function(m, name) {
  s <- summary(m)
  tibble(
    window   = "0–4h",
    model    = name,
    n        = nobs(m),
    adj_R2   = unname(s$r.sq),
    dev_expl = unname(s$dev.expl),
    fREML    = m$gcv.ubre
  )
}

models_do2_summary_4h <- bind_rows(
  model_row(m0_do2_4h, "m0: s(rel_h)"),
  model_row(m1_do2_4h, "m1: + Sponge RE"),
  model_row(m2_do2_4h, "m2: + Sponge RE + Osculum RE"),
  model_row(m3_do2_4h, "m3: + Osculum RE + fs(rel_h,Sponge)")
)

cat("\n--- Model fit summary table (0–4h) ---\n")
print(models_do2_summary_4h)

write.csv(models_do2_summary_4h, file.path(out_dir, "models_DO2_summary_0_4h.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Smooth-term table for time-related terms
# ---------------------------------------------------------
smooth_table <- function(m, model_name) {
  st <- as.data.frame(summary(m)$s.table)
  st$term  <- rownames(st)
  st$model <- model_name
  rownames(st) <- NULL
  st
}

st_time_4h <- bind_rows(
  smooth_table(m0_do2_4h, "m0"),
  smooth_table(m1_do2_4h, "m1"),
  smooth_table(m2_do2_4h, "m2"),
  smooth_table(m3_do2_4h, "m3")
) %>%
  filter(grepl("^s\\(rel_h", term))

cat("\n--- Smooth terms related to time since feeding (0–4h) ---\n")
print(st_time_4h)

write.csv(st_time_4h, file.path(out_dir, "smooth_terms_DO2_time_0_4h.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6) k-check (quick diagnostics)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- gam.check (0–4h) ---\n")
gam.check(m0_do2_4h)
gam.check(m3_do2_4h)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7) OPTIONAL rho estimation within 0–4h (only if you insist)
#    This can be less stable than 0–12h, so treat as QC not "truth".
# ---------------------------------------------------------
estimate_rho_4h <- FALSE

if (estimate_rho_4h) {

  lag1_cor <- function(x) {
    x <- x[is.finite(x)]
    if (length(x) < 3) return(NA_real_)
    cor(x[-length(x)], x[-1])
  }

  ord_var <- if (has_rel_sec) "rel_sec" else "rel_h"

  df_res <- df_do2_4h %>%
    arrange(.data$Osculum_ID, .data[[ord_var]]) %>%
    mutate(resid = residuals(m3_do2_4h, type = "response"))

  rho_by <- df_res %>%
    group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
    summarise(rho_lag1 = lag1_cor(resid), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

  rho_hat_4h <- median(rho_by$rho_lag1, na.rm = TRUE)
  rho_hat_4h <- max(min(rho_hat_4h, 0.95), 0)

  cat("\n--- OPTIONAL rho_hat_4h ---\n")
  print(summary(rho_by$rho_lag1))
  cat("rho_hat_4h =", round(rho_hat_4h, 3), "\n")

  # if you want, refit final:
  m3_do2_4h_final <- bam(
    dO2 ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
    data = df_do2_4h, method = "fREML", discrete = TRUE,
    rho = rho_hat_4h, AR.start = df_do2_4h$AR_start
  )

  # keep as separate object (do not overwrite)
  m_DO2_4h_final <- m3_do2_4h_final
  rm(m3_do2_4h_final)
} else {
  m_DO2_4h_final <- m3_do2_4h
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Partial effects figure (ggplot A+B), excluding Osculum RE in prediction
# ---------------------------------------------------------
grid <- expand.grid(
  rel_h      = seq(0, 4, length.out = 250),
  Sponge_cod = levels(df_do2_4h$Sponge_cod),
  Osculum_ID = levels(df_do2_4h$Osculum_ID)[1]
)

pr <- predict(
  m_DO2_4h_final,
  newdata = grid,
  type = "terms",
  se.fit = TRUE,
  exclude = "s(Osculum_ID)"
)

term_names  <- colnames(pr$fit)
term_global <- term_names[grepl("^s\\(rel_h\\)$", term_names)]
term_fs     <- term_names[grepl("s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)", term_names)]

stopifnot(length(term_global) == 1, length(term_fs) == 1)

# Panel A (global)
dfA <- as_tibble(grid) %>%
  mutate(term = pr$fit[, term_global], se = pr$se.fit[, term_global]) %>%
  filter(Sponge_cod == levels(df_do2_4h$Sponge_cod)[1]) %>%
  mutate(lo = term - 1.96 * se, hi = term + 1.96 * se)

pA <- ggplot(dfA, aes(rel_h, term)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:4, limits = c(0, 4)) +
  labs(title = "A", x = "Time since feeding (h)", y = expression("Partial effect on "~Delta*O[2])) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Panel B (fs deviations)
dfB <- as_tibble(grid) %>%
  mutate(dev = pr$fit[, term_fs])

pB <- ggplot(dfB, aes(rel_h, dev, color = Sponge_cod, group = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:4, limits = c(0, 4)) +
  labs(title = "B", x = "Time since feeding (h)",
       y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)", color = "Sponge") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig_4h <- pA + pB + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.15))
fig_4h

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_0_4h.png"),
  plot = fig_4h, width = 12.5, height = 4.8, dpi = 300
)

cat("\nSaved:\n  ", file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_0_4h.png"), "\n")

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

```


### Extract results from ΔO2 models (mgcv::bam/gam): edf / F / p + adj.R2 + deviance explained + n + model comparisons
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# Extract results from ΔO2 models (mgcv::bam/gam):
#   edf / F / p + adj.R2 + deviance explained + n + model comparisons
# ==================================================================================================
#
# EXPECTED OBJECTS (canonical names)
#   0–12 h: m0_do2, m1_do2, m2_do2, m3_do2, m4_do2 (optionally m4_do2_final)
#   0–4  h: m0_do2_4h, m1_do2_4h, m2_do2_4h, m3_do2_4h (optionally m_DO2_4h_final)
#
# Notes
#   - Works for bam() and gam() objects (both have summary() with s.table).
#   - For bs="re" terms, p-values exist but inference should rely on anova() comparisons.
#   - Term-matching is robust to ordering and mgcv version column naming.
####################################################################################################

library(mgcv)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: core extraction from mgcv::summary
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_gam_core <- function(mod, model_name) {
  sm <- summary(mod)

  s_tbl <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  s_tbl$term <- rownames(s_tbl)
  rownames(s_tbl) <- NULL

  # normalize p column naming across mgcv versions
  if ("p-value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p-value"] <- "p_value"
  if ("p.value" %in% names(s_tbl)) names(s_tbl)[names(s_tbl) == "p.value"] <- "p_value"

  list(
    model     = model_name,
    n         = nobs(mod),
    adj_r2    = unname(sm$r.sq),
    dev_expl  = unname(sm$dev.expl),  # proportion (0–1)
    s_table   = s_tbl
  )
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: pick terms by pattern
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_gam_terms <- function(mod, model_name,
                             term_patterns = c(
                               global_time = "^s\\(rel_h\\)$",
                               sponge_fs   = "s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)",
                               sponge_re   = "s\\(Sponge_cod\\)",
                               osculum_re  = "s\\(Osculum_ID\\)"
                             )) {

  core <- extract_gam_core(mod, model_name)
  st   <- core$s_table

  grab <- function(pattern) {
    ii <- grep(pattern, st$term)
    if (!length(ii)) return(tibble(edf = NA_real_, F = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
    i <- ii[1]
    tibble(
      edf = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
      F   = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
      p   = as.numeric(if ("p_value" %in% names(st)) st$p_value[i] else NA_real_)
    )
  }

  t_global <- grab(term_patterns["global_time"])
  t_fs     <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_fs"])
  t_sre    <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_re"])
  t_ore    <- grab(term_patterns["osculum_re"])

  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    n = core$n,
    adj_R2 = core$adj_r2,
    dev_expl_pct = 100 * core$dev_expl,

    global_edf = t_global$edf, global_F = t_global$F, global_p = t_global$p,
    fs_edf     = t_fs$edf,     fs_F     = t_fs$F,     fs_p     = t_fs$p,

    spongeRE_edf = t_sre$edf, spongeRE_F = t_sre$F, spongeRE_p = t_sre$p,
    osculumRE_edf = t_ore$edf, osculumRE_F = t_ore$F, osculumRE_p = t_ore$p
  )
}

# ==================================================================================
# 1) 0–12 h summaries (canonical)
# ==================================================================================
stopifnot(exists("m0_do2"), exists("m1_do2"), exists("m2_do2"), exists("m3_do2"), exists("m4_do2"))

gam_12h_list <- list(
  m0_do2 = m0_do2,
  m1_do2 = m1_do2,
  m2_do2 = m2_do2,
  m3_do2 = m3_do2,
  m4_do2 = m4_do2
)

# if you created a final refit with estimated rho, include it too
if (exists("m4_do2_final")) gam_12h_list$m4_do2_final <- m4_do2_final

summary_do2_12h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_12h_list), function(nm) {
  extract_gam_terms(gam_12h_list[[nm]], model_name = nm)
}))

summary_do2_12h_round <- summary_do2_12h %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

cat("\n====================\nΔO2 SUMMARY: 0–12 h\n====================\n")
print(summary_do2_12h_round)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0–12 h model comparisons you likely discuss (structure)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS: ΔO2 0–12 h\n====================\n")
print(anova(m0_do2, m1_do2, test = "F"))  # sponge baseline
print(anova(m1_do2, m2_do2, test = "F"))  # fs vs sponge baseline (if that's your nesting)
print(anova(m1_do2, m3_do2, test = "F"))  # add osculum RE (if nested)
print(anova(m3_do2, m4_do2, test = "F"))  # add fs on top of osculum RE

# If you want "same rho" comparison (recommended inference for fs), do it explicitly:
# (Assumes you already created the *_same_rho versions; otherwise skip.)
if (exists("m3_same_rho") && exists("m4_same_rho")) {
  cat("\n--- Same-rho comparison (m3_same_rho vs m4_same_rho) ---\n")
  print(anova(m3_same_rho, m4_same_rho, test = "F"))
}

# ==================================================================================
# 2) 0–4 h summaries (canonical)
# ==================================================================================
stopifnot(exists("m0_do2_4h"), exists("m1_do2_4h"), exists("m2_do2_4h"))

gam_4h_list <- list(
  m0_do2_4h = m0_do2_4h,
  m1_do2_4h = m1_do2_4h,
  m2_do2_4h = m2_do2_4h
)

# if you have the fs model in 0–4h, include it:
if (exists("m3_do2_4h")) gam_4h_list$m3_do2_4h <- m3_do2_4h
# if you stored a canonical final:
if (exists("m_DO2_4h_final")) gam_4h_list$m_DO2_4h_final <- m_DO2_4h_final

summary_do2_4h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_4h_list), function(nm) {
  extract_gam_terms(gam_4h_list[[nm]], model_name = nm)
}))

summary_do2_4h_round <- summary_do2_4h %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

cat("\n====================\nΔO2 SUMMARY: 0–4 h\n====================\n")
print(summary_do2_4h_round)

cat("\n====================\nMODEL COMPARISONS: ΔO2 0–4 h\n====================\n")
print(anova(m0_do2_4h, m1_do2_4h, test = "F"))
print(anova(m1_do2_4h, m2_do2_4h, test = "F"))
if (exists("m3_do2_4h")) print(anova(m2_do2_4h, m3_do2_4h, test = "F"))

# ==================================================================================
# 3) Text-ready extract (pick what you report as "final")
# ==================================================================================
pick_name <- if (exists("m4_do2_final")) "m4_do2_final" else "m4_do2"

pick <- summary_do2_12h %>% filter(model == pick_name)

cat("\n=== TEXT-READY (ΔO2, 0–12 h) ===\n")
cat("model = ", pick_name, "\n", sep = "")
cat("n = ", pick$n, "\n", sep = "")
cat("global smooth: edf = ", round(pick$global_edf, 2),
    ", F = ", round(pick$global_F, 2),
    ", p = ", signif(pick$global_p, 3), "\n", sep = "")
cat("sponge-specific deviations (fs): edf = ", round(pick$fs_edf, 2),
    ", F = ", round(pick$fs_F, 2),
    ", p = ", signif(pick$fs_p, 3), "\n", sep = "")
cat("adjusted R2 = ", round(pick$adj_R2, 3),
    "; deviance explained = ", round(pick$dev_expl_pct, 2), "%\n", sep = "")

# ==================================================================================
# 4) Export CSVs (reproducibility)
# ==================================================================================
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

write.csv(summary_do2_12h,
          file.path(out_dir, "GAM_DO2_feeding_0_12h_model_summaries.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(summary_do2_4h,
          file.path(out_dir, "GAM_DO2_feeding_0_4h_model_summaries.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nSaved:\n  ",
    file.path(out_dir, "GAM_DO2_feeding_0_12h_model_summaries.csv"), "\n  ",
    file.path(out_dir, "GAM_DO2_feeding_0_4h_model_summaries.csv"), "\n", sep = "")
####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################


```


### Paired (0–30 vs 30–60 min) + per-osculum Spearman (0–12h & 0–4h) — ΔO₂
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ==================================================================================================
# ΔO2 additional statistics (canonical + robust)
# Source of truth:
#   - df_do2_12h (preferred; built earlier)
#   - df_do2_4h  (optional; if exists, use it for the 0–4h Spearman window)
#
# This chunk:
#   1) Builds do2_clean from df_do2_12h (finite dO2, rel_h, rel_sec)
#   2) Coverage check for first hour: 0–30 vs 30–60 min per Osculum_ID
#   3) Paired Wilcoxon on per-osculum bin means (uses complete pairs only; no stop())
#   4) Spearman per Osculum_ID (dO2 vs rel_h) for 0–12h and 0–4h with BH-FDR
#
# Outputs (objects):
#   do2_clean, do2_coverage_0_60
#   do2_paired, do2_wilcox, do2_paired_summary, p_do2_paired
#   sp_do2_12h, sp_do2_4h, sp_do2_all, p_do2_rho_hist, p_do2_rho_by_sponge
# ==================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# =========================
# 0) Input checks
# =========================
stopifnot(exists("df_do2_12h"))

REQ <- c("dO2", "rel_h", "rel_sec", "Sponge_cod", "Osculum_ID")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(df_do2_12h))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("df_do2_12h is missing: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

# =========================
# 1) Clean canonical dataset (0–12 h)
# =========================
do2_clean <- df_do2_12h %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(dO2), is.finite(rel_h), is.finite(rel_sec),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID),
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod)
  )

cat("ΔO2 clean dataset (do2_clean):\n")
cat("  N rows:", nrow(do2_clean), "\n")
cat("  Osculum series:", n_distinct(do2_clean$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("  Sponges:", n_distinct(do2_clean$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")

# =========================
# 2) Coverage check for first hour (0–30 vs 30–60 min)
# =========================
do2_coverage_0_60 <- do2_clean %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h < 1) %>%
  mutate(bin = if_else(rel_h < 0.5, "bin_0_30", "bin_30_60")) %>%
  count(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, bin, name = "n_points") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = bin, values_from = n_points, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(ok_pair = (bin_0_30 > 0) & (bin_30_60 > 0)) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID)

cat("Coverage check (first hour):\n")
print(do2_coverage_0_60 %>% count(ok_pair))

if (any(!do2_coverage_0_60$ok_pair)) {
  cat("\nOscula missing one of the bins (listing):\n")
  print(do2_coverage_0_60 %>% filter(!ok_pair), n = Inf)
}
cat("\n")

# =========================
# 3) Paired test (per-osculum mean ΔO2; complete pairs only)
# =========================
do2_paired_long <- do2_clean %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h < 1) %>%
  mutate(bin = if_else(rel_h < 0.5, "t0_0_30min", "t30_30_60min")) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, bin) %>%
  summarise(
    dO2_mean = mean(dO2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_points = n(),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

# Wide (CRITICAL: id_cols fixed so n_points doesn't become part of ID)
do2_paired <- do2_paired_long %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = c(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod),
    names_from  = bin,
    values_from = c(dO2_mean, n_points),
    values_fill = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(dO2_mean_t0_0_30min), !is.na(dO2_mean_t30_30_60min)) %>%
  mutate(delta = dO2_mean_t30_30_60min - dO2_mean_t0_0_30min)

cat("Paired dataset (do2_paired):\n")
cat("  Rows (pairs):", nrow(do2_paired), "\n")
cat("  Unique Osculum_ID:", n_distinct(do2_paired$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

do2_wilcox <- NULL
if (nrow(do2_paired) >= 2) {
  do2_wilcox <- wilcox.test(
    do2_paired$dO2_mean_t0_0_30min,
    do2_paired$dO2_mean_t30_30_60min,
    paired = TRUE,
    exact  = FALSE
  )
  cat("Wilcoxon paired test (ΔO2, 0–30 vs 30–60 min):\n")
  print(do2_wilcox)
} else {
  cat("Wilcoxon skipped: <2 complete pairs.\n")
}

do2_paired_summary <- do2_paired %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs     = n(),
    median_t0   = median(dO2_mean_t0_0_30min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_t30  = median(dO2_mean_t30_30_60min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_diff   = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_diff     = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\nPaired summary (ΔO2):\n")
print(do2_paired_summary)
cat("\n")

p_do2_paired <- do2_paired %>%
  select(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, dO2_mean_t0_0_30min, dO2_mean_t30_30_60min) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(dO2_mean_t0_0_30min, dO2_mean_t30_30_60min),
    names_to = "time_bin",
    values_to = "dO2_mean"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    time_bin = factor(
      time_bin,
      levels = c("dO2_mean_t0_0_30min", "dO2_mean_t30_30_60min"),
      labels = c("0–30 min", "30–60 min")
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(time_bin, dO2_mean, group = Osculum_ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(Delta*O[2]~(mu*mol~O[2]~L^{-1})),
    title = expression("Paired comparison (within Osculum_ID): 0–30 vs 30–60 min post-feeding ("*Delta*O[2]*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic()

print(p_do2_paired)

# =========================
# 4) Spearman per Osculum_ID (ΔO2 vs rel_h)
# =========================
safe_spearman <- function(x, y, min_n = 4) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y) & !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
  x <- x[ok]; y <- y[ok]
  if (length(x) < min_n) return(list(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = length(x), ok = FALSE, reason = "n<min"))
  if (sd(x) == 0 || sd(y) == 0) return(list(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = length(x), ok = FALSE, reason = "sd=0"))
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  list(rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, n = length(x), ok = TRUE, reason = NA_character_)
}

spearman_by_osculum <- function(df, window_label, min_n = 4) {
  res <- df %>%
    group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod) %>%
    summarise(
      tmp = list(safe_spearman(dO2, rel_h, min_n = min_n)),
      n   = tmp[[1]]$n,
      rho = tmp[[1]]$rho,
      p   = tmp[[1]]$p,
      ok  = tmp[[1]]$ok,
      reason = tmp[[1]]$reason,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(-tmp) %>%
    mutate(
      window = window_label,
      p_fdr  = ifelse(is.na(p), NA_real_, p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))
    )

  cat("Spearman diagnostics:", window_label, "\n")
  cat("  Total series:", nrow(res), "\n")
  cat("  Valid tests:", sum(res$ok, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("  Excluded (n<min):", sum(res$reason == "n<min", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("  Excluded (sd=0):", sum(res$reason == "sd=0", na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")
  res
}

# 0–12h (use do2_clean)
sp_do2_12h <- spearman_by_osculum(do2_clean %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 12), "0-12h")

# 0–4h (prefer df_do2_4h if exists, else cut from do2_clean)
if (exists("df_do2_4h")) {
  miss4 <- setdiff(c("dO2","rel_h","Sponge_cod","Osculum_ID"), names(df_do2_4h))
  if (length(miss4) == 0) {
    do2_4h_clean <- df_do2_4h %>%
      filter(is.finite(dO2), is.finite(rel_h), !is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod)) %>%
      mutate(Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID), Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod))
    sp_do2_4h <- spearman_by_osculum(do2_4h_clean, "0-4h")
  } else {
    sp_do2_4h <- spearman_by_osculum(do2_clean %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4), "0-4h")
  }
} else {
  sp_do2_4h <- spearman_by_osculum(do2_clean %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4), "0-4h")
}

sp_do2_all <- bind_rows(sp_do2_12h, sp_do2_4h)

cat("Spearman per Osculum_ID summary (ΔO2):\n")
print(
  sp_do2_all %>%
    group_by(window) %>%
    summarise(
      n_series      = n(),
      n_valid       = sum(!is.na(rho)),
      n_sig_p05     = sum(p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_sig_fdr05   = sum(p_fdr < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_rho    = median(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_rho      = mean(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups       = "drop"
    )
)

# Plots
p_do2_rho_hist <- ggplot(sp_do2_all %>% filter(!is.na(rho)), aes(rho)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Spearman "*rho*" ("*Delta*O[2]*" vs time since feeding)"),
    y = "Count",
    title = expression("Distribution of per-osculum Spearman correlations ("*Delta*O[2]*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic()
print(p_do2_rho_hist)

p_do2_rho_by_sponge <- ggplot(sp_do2_all %>% filter(!is.na(rho)), aes(Sponge_cod, rho)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0)) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Sponge",
    y = expression("Spearman "*rho),
    title = expression("Per-osculum "*rho*" values by sponge ("*Delta*O[2]*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic()
print(p_do2_rho_by_sponge)

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################




```

### Synchrony in oxygen removal (ΔO2) between paired sponges:Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

################################################################################
# Synchrony in oxygen removal (ΔO2) between paired sponges
# Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot)
#
# INPUT:
#   df_base must include: Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Sponge_cod, ΔO2
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   1) df_pairwide_do2       : paired-wide table (A/B) per timestamp and run
#   2) cor_pair_by_run_do2   : Spearman rho per Pair_ID × Repeat_Index
#   3) p_do2_box             : boxplot of rho distributions per Pair_ID
#   4) summary_DO2           : mean/median rho per pair + Overall
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# USER SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
use_median_agg <- TRUE   # if duplicates exist per sponge×time: median (robust) or mean
min_nDO2       <- 9      # keep only runs with >= 9 paired timestamps

# Colors per pair (consistent across figures)
pair_colors <- c(
  "82-83" = "#FF69B4",
  "84-85" = "#7A378B",
  "86-87" = "#104E8B"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: safe aggregation (ignores non-finite)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
agg_fun_safe <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  if (use_median_agg) median(x) else mean(x)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1 — Clean + keep only finite ΔO2 rows
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_do2 <- df_base %>%
  mutate(
    Repeat_Index  = as.integer(Repeat_Index),
    Sponge_cod    = as.character(Sponge_cod),
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    DO2           = as.numeric(`ΔO2`)   # rename for convenience (avoid special character)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(DO2))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2 — Aggregate duplicates: one DO2 per (run × time × sponge)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_do2_agg <- df_do2 %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Sponge_cod) %>%
  summarise(DO2 = agg_fun_safe(DO2), .groups = "drop")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3 — Keep only true parallel measurements (exactly two sponges at that time)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_do2_keep2 <- df_do2_agg %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Sponge_cod) == 2) %>%
  ungroup()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4 — Build paired-wide table (A/B) for ΔO2
#   A = smaller Sponge_cod, B = larger Sponge_cod (stable biological definition)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_pairwide_do2 <- df_do2_keep2 %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  summarise(
    A_id    = min(Sponge_cod),
    B_id    = max(Sponge_cod),
    Pair_ID = paste(A_id, B_id, sep = "-"),
    A       = DO2[Sponge_cod == A_id][1],
    B       = DO2[Sponge_cod == B_id][1],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  select(Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Pair_ID, A, B) %>%
  arrange(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Pair_ID)

# Optional quick check
# str(df_pairwide_do2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5 — Spearman correlation within each run (Pair_ID × Repeat_Index)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cor_pair_by_run_do2 <- df_pairwide_do2 %>%
  group_by(Pair_ID, Repeat_Index) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points   = n(),
    spearman_r = if (n_points >= min_nDO2)
      cor(A, B, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
    else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(spearman_r))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 6 — Summary table: per pair + Overall (mean + median)
#   mean / median Spearman rho + 95% bootstrap CI half-width (±CI)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

bootstrap_ci_halfwidth <- function(x, conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 3) return(NA_real_)

  boot_means <- replicate(
    nboot,
    mean(sample(x, replace = TRUE))
  )

  alpha <- (1 - conf) / 2
  ci <- quantile(boot_means, probs = c(alpha, 1 - alpha), names = FALSE)

  (ci[2] - ci[1]) / 2
}


summarise_cor_table <- function(cor_by_run, r_col = "spearman_r",
                                conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {

  stopifnot(all(c("Pair_ID", "Repeat_Index", r_col) %in% names(cor_by_run)))

  per_pair <- cor_by_run %>%
    group_by(Pair_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(Pair_ID)

  overall <- cor_by_run %>%
    summarise(
      Pair_ID  = "Overall",
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot)
    )

  bind_rows(per_pair, overall)
}

summary_DO2 <- summarise_cor_table(
  cor_pair_by_run_do2,
  r_col = "spearman_r"
)

summary_DO2

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 7 — Publication-style boxplot (white background, no grid)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_do2_box <- ggplot(cor_pair_by_run_do2, aes(x = Pair_ID, y = spearman_r, fill = Pair_ID)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    width = 0.6,
    outlier.shape = 21,
    outlier.size = 2,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.12,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pair_colors) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 15) +
  labs(
    title    = expression("Within-run synchrony in oxygen removal (" * Delta * "O"[2] * ")"),
    subtitle = "Spearman rank correlation between paired sponges, calculated separately for each run",
    x = "Sponge pair",
    y = expression("Spearman correlation (" * rho * ")")
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13)
  )

p_do2_box

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 8 — Save figure
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Spearman_boxplot_pairs_DO2.png",
  plot     = p_do2_box,
  path     = "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R",
  width    = 7,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white"
)


```

## Respiration 

### Respiration diel analysis (24 h): GAMs + extract numbers for Results text
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
### Respiration diel analysis (24 h): GAMs + extract numbers for Results text
# ==================================================================================================
# Respiration diel analysis (24h): fit + plot + extract numbers for Results
# Canonical version (does NOT overwrite base tables)
# Mirrors OFR diel chunk structure
# ==================================================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)

# -------------------------
# 0) Inputs (canonical)
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base") | exists("Sep_2024_Data"))

df0 <- if (exists("iv_feeding_base")) iv_feeding_base else Sep_2024_Data

# output folder (single source of truth)
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# 0.1) Hard check: Respiration exists
# -------------------------
if (!("Respiration" %in% names(df0))) {
  stop("Column 'Respiration' not found in df0. Available columns:\n",
       paste(names(df0), collapse = ", "))
}

# -------------------------
# 1) Build diel dataset (Respiration only; no feeding alignment)
# -------------------------
# Robust osculum identifier: prefer Repeat_Code if present, else Repeat_Index
rep_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Repeat_Code"  %in% names(df0) ~ "Repeat_Code",
  "Repeat_Index" %in% names(df0) ~ "Repeat_Index",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(rep_col)) stop("Need Repeat_Code or Repeat_Index to construct Osculum_ID.")

df_resp_diel <- df0 %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Rep_id        = as.character(.data[[rep_col]]),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Rep_id, sep = "_")),
    hour          = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")),
    DayNight      = factor(ifelse(hour >= 6 & hour < 18, "Day", "Night"),
                           levels = c("Day", "Night")),
    tod           = as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%H")) +
                    as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%M"))/60 +
                    as.numeric(format(Date_and_time, "%S"))/3600,
    Respiration   = as.numeric(Respiration)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(Respiration),
    !is.na(Date_and_time),
    is.finite(tod), tod >= 0, tod < 24,
    !is.na(Sponge_cod),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("Diel Respiration dataset:\n")
cat("n obs =", nrow(df_resp_diel), "\n")
cat("n sponges =", n_distinct(df_resp_diel$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("n oscula  =", n_distinct(df_resp_diel$Osculum_ID), "\n")
print(table(df_resp_diel$Sponge_cod))
print(table(df_resp_diel$DayNight))

# -------------------------
# 2) Fit models
# -------------------------
# A) population cyclic smooth
m_resp_tod <- gam(
  Respiration ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8),
  data   = df_resp_diel,
  method = "REML",
  knots  = list(tod = c(0, 24))
)

# B) binary day/night as LM (so you get Estimate ± SE, t, p)
m_resp_dn_lm <- lm(Respiration ~ DayNight, data = df_resp_diel)

# C) population cyclic + sponge-specific deviations (factor-smooth interaction)
m_resp_sponge <- gam(
  Respiration ~ s(tod, bs = "cc", k = 8) + s(tod, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
  data   = df_resp_diel,
  method = "REML",
  knots  = list(tod = c(0, 24))
)

# -------------------------
# 3) Extract numbers for Results text
# -------------------------
extract_smooth <- function(mod, pattern) {
  sm <- summary(mod)
  st <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  st$term <- rownames(st); rownames(st) <- NULL
  pcol <- intersect(names(st), c("p-value", "p.value"))
  if (length(pcol) == 1) names(st)[names(st) == pcol] <- "p_value"

  i <- grep(pattern, st$term)
  if (length(i) == 0) return(data.frame(term=NA, edf=NA, F=NA, p=NA))
  i <- i[1]
  data.frame(
    term = st$term[i],
    edf  = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
    F    = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
    p    = as.numeric(st$p_value[i])
  )
}

A_sum <- summary(m_resp_tod)
C_sum <- summary(m_resp_sponge)

A_s_tod <- extract_smooth(m_resp_tod, "s\\(tod\\)")
C_s_tod <- extract_smooth(m_resp_sponge, "s\\(tod\\)")
C_s_fs  <- extract_smooth(m_resp_sponge, "s\\(tod,Sponge_cod\\)")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL A (population cyclic smooth)\n============================\n")
cat("s(tod): edf=", round(A_s_tod$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(A_s_tod$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(A_s_tod$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("adjusted R2=", round(A_sum$r.sq, 4),
    "; deviance explained=", round(100*A_sum$dev.expl, 2), "%\n", sep="")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL B (Day vs Night; LM)\n============================\n")
lm_sum <- summary(m_resp_dn_lm)
coef_tab <- coef(lm_sum)
dn_row <- grep("^DayNight", rownames(coef_tab))
if (length(dn_row) > 0) {
  dn_row <- dn_row[1]
  cat("DayNight effect: estimate=",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"Estimate"], 3), " ± ",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"Std. Error"], 3), " SE, t=",
      round(coef_tab[dn_row,"t value"], 2), ", p=",
      signif(coef_tab[dn_row,"Pr(>|t|)"], 3), "\n", sep="")
} else {
  cat("DayNight coefficient not found (check factor coding).\n")
}
cat("R2=", round(lm_sum$r.squared, 4),
    "; adjusted R2=", round(lm_sum$adj.r.squared, 4), "\n", sep="")

cat("\n============================\nMODEL C (population + sponge-specific deviations)\n============================\n")
cat("global s(tod): edf=", round(C_s_tod$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(C_s_tod$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(C_s_tod$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("sponge-specific s(tod,Sponge_cod): edf=", round(C_s_fs$edf, 2),
    ", F=", round(C_s_fs$F, 2),
    ", p=", signif(C_s_fs$p, 3), "\n", sep="")
cat("adjusted R2=", round(C_sum$r.sq, 4),
    "; deviance explained=", round(100*C_sum$dev.expl, 2), "%\n", sep="")

# -------------------------
# 4) Figure (A|B) with fixed sponge colors (gratia)
# -------------------------
library(gratia)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# canonical palette for the whole chapter (reuse if already exists)
if (!exists("sponge_palette")) {
  sponge_palette <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# A: population smooth
pA <- draw(
  m_resp_tod,
  select = "s(tod)",
  rug = FALSE
) +
  labs(
    title = "A",
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Partial effect on respiration"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, 6), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

# B: sponge-specific deviations (colored)
pB <- draw(
  m_resp_sponge,
  select = "s(tod,Sponge_cod)",
  se = FALSE,
  rug = FALSE
) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = sponge_palette, name = "Sponge") +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, 6), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)

p_combined_resp <- (pA | pB) + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_Respiration_diel_GAM_population_and_sponge.png"),
  plot     = p_combined_resp,
  width    = 12,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

p_combined_resp

```


### Respiration response to feeding (0–12 h): build df_12h_resp ONCE + raw plots + QA + GAMs + partial effects
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

### Respiration response to feeding (0–12 h): build df_12h_resp ONCE + raw plots + QA + GAMs + partial effects
## ================================================================================================
## PURPOSE
##   Test whether respiration changes systematically after feeding (t=0) over 0–12 h.
##   - Build df_12h_resp once (aligned to feeding within each Osculum_ID).
##   - RAW plots for supplement/QA (do not infer from raw dense data).
##   - Inference models on BINNED data: one mean per Osculum_ID × 30-min bin.
##
## CANONICAL DEFINITIONS
##   - Osculum_ID = paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep="_")
##   - Feed_time  = earliest Date_and_time with Feeding=="after" within Osculum_ID
##   - rel_min / rel_h = time since feeding
##   - rel_30 = 30-min bins (minutes) for plotting + binning
##
## OUTPUT
##   - df_12h_resp              (raw aligned)
##   - df_12h_resp_binned       (binned for inference)
##   - Models: m0_resp, m1_resp, m2_resp, m3_resp, m4_resp (+ optional m4_resp_k)
##   - Figures saved:
##       Resp_feeding_0_12h_points.png
##       Resp_feeding_0_12h_facets_by_OsculumID.png
##       Fig_Resp_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_m4_0_12h.png
## ================================================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# -------------------------
# 0) Settings
# -------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Keep chapter palette (use existing cols if already defined)
if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# Choose ONE canonical input object
# Prefer iv_feeding_base if you already standardized it; otherwise fall back to Sep_2024_Data.
if (exists("iv_feeding_base")) {
  dat0 <- iv_feeding_base
} else if (exists("Sep_2024_Data")) {
  dat0 <- Sep_2024_Data
} else {
  stop("Need iv_feeding_base or Sep_2024_Data in the environment.")
}

REQ <- c("Taxa_cod","Date_and_time","Feeding","Sponge_cod","Repeat_Code","Respiration")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(dat0))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("Input data missing columns: ", paste(miss, collapse=", "))

# -------------------------
# 1) Build df_12h_resp ONCE (raw aligned data)
# -------------------------
df0_resp <- dat0 %>%
  filter(Taxa_cod == "I.variabilis") %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),               # keep existing tz convention from your pipeline
    Respiration   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Respiration)),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod, levels = names(cols)),
    Repeat_Code   = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID    = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_"))
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Respiration), !is.na(Date_and_time), !is.na(Osculum_ID)) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, Date_and_time)

# Align to feeding time per Osculum_ID
df_rel_resp <- df0_resp %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code) %>%
  mutate(
    Feed_time = {
      x <- Date_and_time[Feeding == "after"]
      if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) as.POSIXct(NA) else min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    },
    rel_min = as.numeric(difftime(Date_and_time, Feed_time, units = "mins"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(Feed_time), is.finite(rel_min))

df_12h_resp <- df_rel_resp %>%
  filter(rel_min >= 0, rel_min <= 12*60) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_h  = rel_min / 60,
    rel_30 = floor(rel_min / 30) * 30
  )

cat("df_12h_resp\n")
cat("  rows:   ", nrow(df_12h_resp), "\n")
cat("  sponges:", n_distinct(df_12h_resp$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df_12h_resp$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# OPTIONAL: if you *know* specific series are bad for respiration too, filter them here.
# df_12h_resp <- df_12h_resp %>% filter(!Osculum_ID %in% c("82_2_A","83_2_B"))

# -------------------------
# 2) RAW plots (supplement/QA) — do not use raw for inference
# -------------------------
p_resp_points <- ggplot(df_12h_resp, aes(rel_h, Respiration)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = as.character(Sponge_cod)), alpha = 0.25, size = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = expression("Respiration"~(mu*mol~O[2]~h^{-1})),
    color = "Sponge"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

p_resp_facets <- ggplot(df_12h_resp, aes(x = rel_30, y = Respiration, group = Osculum_ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(aes(color = as.character(Sponge_cod)), alpha = 0.7, size = 1.1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 720, 120),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x/60, " h")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Time since feeding",
    y = expression("Respiration"~(mu*mol~O[2]~h^{-1})),
    color = "Sponge"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Osculum_ID, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic()

p_resp_points
p_resp_facets

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Resp_feeding_0_12h_points.png"), p_resp_points, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Resp_feeding_0_12h_facets_by_OsculumID.png"), p_resp_facets, width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300)

# -------------------------
# 3) Minimal QA (optional but useful)
# -------------------------
qa <- df_12h_resp %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points = n(),
    n_bins   = n_distinct(rel_30),
    has_bin0 = any(rel_30 == 0),
    min_rel  = min(rel_min),
    max_rel  = max(rel_min),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(has_bin0, n_bins)

cat("QA: has t=0 bin?\n")
print(table(qa$has_bin0))
cat("\nQA: n_bins summary\n")
print(summary(qa$n_bins))
cat("\nSparse series (n_bins < 5):\n")
print(qa %>% filter(n_bins < 5), n = Inf)

# -------------------------
# 4) Bin aggregation for inference (avoid overweighting dense series)
#    One mean per Osculum_ID × rel_30 (i.e., per 30-min bin)
# -------------------------
df_12h_resp_binned <- df_12h_resp %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, rel_30) %>%
  summarise(
    Respiration = mean(Respiration, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_in_bin    = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(rel_h = rel_30 / 60) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_h) %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod, levels = names(cols)),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("\nRAW rows (0–12h):   ", nrow(df_12h_resp), "\n")
cat("BINNED rows (0–12h):", nrow(df_12h_resp_binned), "\n")
cat("Median n_in_bin:     ", median(df_12h_resp_binned$n_in_bin), "\n\n")

dat_mod <- df_12h_resp_binned

# ================================================================================================
# 5) GAM models (0–12 h) on BINNED data (REML)
# ================================================================================================

m0_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 6),
               data = dat_mod, method = "REML")

m1_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
               data = dat_mod, method = "REML")

m2_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
               data = dat_mod, method = "REML")

m3_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
               data = dat_mod, method = "REML")

m4_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 6) + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
               data = dat_mod, method = "REML")

cat("\n--- Model comparisons (0–12h, binned) ---\n")
print(anova(m0_resp, m1_resp, test="F"))
print(anova(m1_resp, m3_resp, test="F"))
print(anova(m3_resp, m4_resp, test="F"))

cat("\n--- Summaries (m4 is usually your 'final' structure) ---\n")
print(summary(m4_resp))

# Optional: higher-k sensitivity check (only if you want to de-risk k-index)
m4_resp_k <- gam(
  Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 12) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 10),
  data = dat_mod, method = "REML"
)
cat("\n--- Sensitivity (higher k) ---\n")
print(summary(m4_resp_k))

# ================================================================================================
# 6) Main figure: GAM partial effects (A+B) from m4_resp
#    A = population smooth s(rel_h) (exclude osculum RE + fs)
#    B = sponge-specific deviations (fs) (exclude osculum RE + global smooth)
# ================================================================================================

grid_h <- seq(0, 12, length.out = 250)
Sponge_levels <- levels(dat_mod$Sponge_cod)
dummy_osculum <- levels(dat_mod$Osculum_ID)[1]

grid <- expand.grid(
  rel_h      = grid_h,
  Sponge_cod = Sponge_levels,
  Osculum_ID = dummy_osculum
)
grid$Sponge_cod <- factor(grid$Sponge_cod, levels = Sponge_levels)
grid$Osculum_ID <- factor(grid$Osculum_ID, levels = levels(dat_mod$Osculum_ID))

pr <- predict(m4_resp, newdata = grid, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE)

term_names <- colnames(pr$fit)
term_global <- term_names[grepl("^s\\(rel_h\\)$", term_names)]
term_fs     <- term_names[grepl("s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)", term_names)]

stopifnot(length(term_global) == 1, length(term_fs) == 1)

# Panel A (plot once; pick first sponge as carrier)
dfA <- grid %>%
  mutate(term = pr$fit[, term_global], se = pr$se.fit[, term_global]) %>%
  filter(Sponge_cod == Sponge_levels[1]) %>%
  mutate(lo = term - 1.96*se, hi = term + 1.96*se)

pA <- ggplot(dfA, aes(rel_h, term)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "A",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = "Partial effect on respiration"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Panel B (fs deviations for each sponge)
dfB <- grid %>% mutate(dev = pr$fit[, term_fs])

pB <- ggplot(dfB, aes(rel_h, dev, color = Sponge_cod, group = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    x = "Time since feeding (h)",
    y = "Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)",
    color = "Sponge"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig_resp_feeding <- (pA + pB) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.15))
fig_resp_feeding

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_Resp_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_m4_0_12h.png"),
  plot     = fig_resp_feeding,
  width    = 12.5,
  height   = 4.8,
  dpi      = 300
)

# ================================================================================================
# End
# ================================================================================================




```

### Respiration response to feeding (0–4 h): binned GAMs + partial-effects figure + exports

```{r, fig.width=10,fig.height=7}
### Respiration response to feeding (0–4 h): binned GAMs + partial-effects figure + exports

library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# -------------------------
# 0) Checks + palette
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("df_12h_resp"))
REQ <- c("Respiration","rel_h","rel_30","Sponge_cod","Osculum_ID")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(df_12h_resp))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("df_12h_resp missing: ", paste(miss, collapse=", "))

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
```
