```{r, fig.width=10,fig.height=7}
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# -------------------------
# 1) RAW subset: 0–4 h (for plots + source)
# -------------------------
df_4h_resp_raw <- df_12h_resp %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Respiration), is.finite(rel_h), is.finite(rel_30)) %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod, levels = names(cols)),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("df_4h_resp_raw\n")
cat("  rows:   ", nrow(df_4h_resp_raw), "\n")
cat("  sponges:", n_distinct(df_4h_resp_raw$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df_4h_resp_raw$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 2) BINNED dataset for inference (one value per Osculum_ID × 30-min bin)
# -------------------------
df_4h_resp_bin <- df_4h_resp_raw %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, rel_30) %>%
  summarise(
    Respiration = mean(Respiration, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_in_bin    = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(rel_h = rel_30 / 60) %>%
  arrange(Osculum_ID, rel_h)

cat("df_4h_resp_bin\n")
cat("  rows:          ", nrow(df_4h_resp_bin), "\n")
cat("  median n_in_bin:", median(df_4h_resp_bin$n_in_bin), "\n\n")

dat_mod_4h <- df_4h_resp_bin

# ================================================================================================
# 3) Models (0–4 h) on BINNED data (REML)
#   Keep structure parallel to 0–12h:
#     m0: s(rel_h)
#     m1: + Sponge RE
#     m2: + Osculum RE
#     m3: + fs deviations (no global smooth)
#     m4: global + Osculum RE + fs deviations   (usually "final")
# ================================================================================================

m0_4h_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 5),
                  data = dat_mod_4h, method = "REML")

m1_4h_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 5) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re"),
                  data = dat_mod_4h, method = "REML")

m2_4h_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 5) + s(Sponge_cod, bs = "re") + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re"),
                  data = dat_mod_4h, method = "REML")

# sponge-specific deviations only (plus osculum baseline)
m3_4h_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
                  data = dat_mod_4h, method = "REML")

# full: global + osculum baseline + fs deviations (recommended)
m4_4h_resp <- gam(Respiration ~ s(rel_h, k = 5) + s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") + s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 5),
                  data = dat_mod_4h, method = "REML")

cat("\n--- Comparisons (0–4h, binned) ---\n")
print(anova(m0_4h_resp, m1_4h_resp, test="F"))
print(anova(m1_4h_resp, m2_4h_resp, test="F"))
print(anova(m2_4h_resp, m4_4h_resp, test="F"))

cat("\n--- Summary (m4_4h_resp) ---\n")
print(summary(m4_4h_resp))

# Optional: k-sensitivity (0–4h is sparse in time bins, so keep k modest)
# Max unique rel_h values is small (30-min bins), so large k will fail.

m4_4h_resp_k <- gam(
  Respiration ~
    s(rel_h, k = 6) +
    s(Osculum_ID, bs = "re") +
    s(rel_h, Sponge_cod, bs = "fs", k = 6),
  data   = dat_mod_4h,
  method = "REML"
)

summary(m4_4h_resp_k)
gam.check(m4_4h_resp_k)


# ================================================================================================
# 4) RAW visualizations (0–4h) — for context only
# ================================================================================================

p_raw_all <- ggplot(df_4h_resp_raw, aes(rel_h, Respiration)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, k = 5), se = TRUE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, 1), limits = c(0, 4)) +
  labs(x="Time since feeding (h)", y=expression("Respiration"~(mu*mol~O[2]~h^{-1}))) +
  theme_classic()

p_raw_by_sponge <- ggplot(df_4h_resp_raw, aes(rel_h, Respiration)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = as.character(Sponge_cod)), alpha = 0.35, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, k = 5), se = TRUE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, 1), limits = c(0, 4)) +
  labs(x="Time since feeding (h)", y=expression("Respiration"~(mu*mol~O[2]~h^{-1})), color="Sponge") +
  theme_classic()

p_raw_all
p_raw_by_sponge

# ================================================================================================
# 5) Main figure (A+B): partial effects from m4_4h_resp
#   A = population smooth s(rel_h) (exclude osculum RE + fs)
#   B = sponge-specific deviations (fs) (exclude osculum RE + global smooth)
# ================================================================================================

grid_h <- seq(0, 4, length.out = 250)
Sponge_levels <- levels(dat_mod_4h$Sponge_cod)
dummy_osculum <- levels(dat_mod_4h$Osculum_ID)[1]

grid <- expand.grid(
  rel_h      = grid_h,
  Sponge_cod = Sponge_levels,
  Osculum_ID = dummy_osculum
)
grid$Sponge_cod <- factor(grid$Sponge_cod, levels = Sponge_levels)
grid$Osculum_ID <- factor(grid$Osculum_ID, levels = levels(dat_mod_4h$Osculum_ID))

pr <- predict(m4_4h_resp, newdata = grid, type="terms", se.fit=TRUE)

term_names  <- colnames(pr$fit)
term_global <- term_names[grepl("^s\\(rel_h\\)$", term_names)]
term_fs     <- term_names[grepl("s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)", term_names)]
stopifnot(length(term_global)==1, length(term_fs)==1)

dfA <- grid %>%
  mutate(term = pr$fit[, term_global], se = pr$se.fit[, term_global]) %>%
  filter(Sponge_cod == Sponge_levels[1]) %>%
  mutate(lo = term - 1.96*se, hi = term + 1.96*se)

pA <- ggplot(dfA, aes(rel_h, term)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=lo, ymax=hi), alpha=0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth=1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,4,1), limits=c(0,4)) +
  labs(title="A", x="Time since feeding (h)", y="Partial effect on respiration") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face="bold"))

dfB <- grid %>% mutate(dev = pr$fit[, term_fs])

pB <- ggplot(dfB, aes(rel_h, dev, color=Sponge_cod, group=Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_line(linewidth=1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop=FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0,4,1), limits=c(0,4)) +
  labs(title="B", x="Time since feeding (h)", y="Sponge-specific deviation (partial effect)", color="Sponge") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face="bold"))

fig_resp_4h <- (pA + pB) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.15))
fig_resp_4h

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_Resp_feeding_GAM_partial_effects_m4_0_4h.png"),
  plot     = fig_resp_4h,
  width    = 12.5,
  height   = 4.8,
  dpi      = 300
)

# Optional: keep a canonical handle
m_resp_4h_final <- m4_4h_resp






```


### Extract results from Respiration models (mgcv::gam): edf / F / p + adj.R2 + deviance explained + n + comparisons
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# -------------------------
# Helper: core extraction
# -------------------------
extract_gam_core <- function(mod, model_name) {
  sm <- summary(mod)

  s_tbl <- as.data.frame(sm$s.table)
  s_tbl$term <- rownames(s_tbl)
  rownames(s_tbl) <- NULL

  s_tbl <- s_tbl %>%
    rename_with(~ gsub("p-value", "p_value", .x)) %>%
    rename_with(~ gsub("p\\.value", "p_value", .x))

  list(
    model     = model_name,
    n         = nobs(mod),
    adj_r2    = unname(sm$r.sq),
    dev_expl  = unname(sm$dev.expl),
    scale_est = unname(sm$scale),
    s_table   = s_tbl
  )
}

extract_gam_terms <- function(mod, model_name,
                             term_patterns = c(
                               global_time = "^s\\(rel_h\\)$",
                               sponge_fs   = "s\\(rel_h,Sponge_cod\\)",
                               sponge_re   = "^s\\(Sponge_cod\\)$",
                               osculum_re  = "^s\\(Osculum_ID\\)$"
                             )) {
  core <- extract_gam_core(mod, model_name)
  st   <- core$s_table

  grab <- function(pattern) {
    ii <- grep(pattern, st$term)
    if (length(ii) == 0) return(tibble(edf=NA_real_, F=NA_real_, p=NA_real_))
    i <- ii[1]
    tibble(
      edf = as.numeric(st$edf[i]),
      F   = as.numeric(if ("F" %in% names(st)) st$F[i] else NA_real_),
      p   = as.numeric(if ("p_value" %in% names(st)) st$p_value[i] else NA_real_)
    )
  }

  t_glob <- grab(term_patterns["global_time"])
  t_fs   <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_fs"])
  t_sre  <- grab(term_patterns["sponge_re"])
  t_ore  <- grab(term_patterns["osculum_re"])

  tibble(
    model = model_name,
    n = core$n,
    adj_R2 = core$adj_r2,
    dev_expl_pct = 100 * core$dev_expl,
    global_edf = t_glob$edf, global_F = t_glob$F, global_p = t_glob$p,
    fs_edf     = t_fs$edf,   fs_F     = t_fs$F,   fs_p     = t_fs$p,
    spongeRE_edf = t_sre$edf, spongeRE_F = t_sre$F, spongeRE_p = t_sre$p,
    osculumRE_edf = t_ore$edf, osculumRE_F = t_ore$F, osculumRE_p = t_ore$p
  )
}

# -------------------------
# 1) 0–12h model list (expects you already ran the 0–12h chunk)
# -------------------------
need_12h <- c("m0_resp","m1_resp","m2_resp","m3_resp","m4_resp")
if (!all(sapply(need_12h, exists))) {
  cat("Missing some 0–12h respiration models. Found:\n")
  print(sapply(need_12h, exists))
} else {
  gam_12h <- list(m0_resp=m0_resp, m1_resp=m1_resp, m2_resp=m2_resp, m3_resp=m3_resp, m4_resp=m4_resp)

  summary_resp_12h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_12h), function(nm) {
    extract_gam_terms(gam_12h[[nm]], nm)
  }))

  summary_resp_12h_round <- summary_resp_12h %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

  cat("\n--- Respiration GAM summaries (0–12h) ---\n")
  print(summary_resp_12h_round)

  cat("\n--- Respiration model comparisons (0–12h) ---\n")
  print(anova(m0_resp, m1_resp, test="F"))
  print(anova(m1_resp, m3_resp, test="F"))
  print(anova(m3_resp, m4_resp, test="F"))

  write.csv(summary_resp_12h, file.path(out_dir, "GAM_Resp_feeding_0_12h_model_summaries.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}

# -------------------------
# 2) 0–4h model list (expects you already ran the 0–4h chunk above)
# -------------------------
need_4h <- c("m0_4h_resp","m1_4h_resp","m2_4h_resp","m3_4h_resp","m4_4h_resp")
if (!all(sapply(need_4h, exists))) {
  cat("\nMissing some 0–4h respiration models. Found:\n")
  print(sapply(need_4h, exists))
} else {
  gam_4h <- list(m0_4h_resp=m0_4h_resp, m1_4h_resp=m1_4h_resp, m2_4h_resp=m2_4h_resp, m3_4h_resp=m3_4h_resp, m4_4h_resp=m4_4h_resp)

  summary_resp_4h <- bind_rows(lapply(names(gam_4h), function(nm) {
    extract_gam_terms(gam_4h[[nm]], nm)
  }))

  summary_resp_4h_round <- summary_resp_4h %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

  cat("\n--- Respiration GAM summaries (0–4h) ---\n")
  print(summary_resp_4h_round)

  cat("\n--- Respiration model comparisons (0–4h) ---\n")
  print(anova(m0_4h_resp, m1_4h_resp, test="F"))
  print(anova(m1_4h_resp, m2_4h_resp, test="F"))
  print(anova(m2_4h_resp, m4_4h_resp, test="F"))

  write.csv(summary_resp_4h, file.path(out_dir, "GAM_Resp_feeding_0_4h_model_summaries.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}





```

### Respiration: Immediate response (0–60 min) + per-osculum monotonic trends (0–12 h / 0–4 h)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ================================================================================================
# Respiration — Immediate response (0–60 min) + per-osculum monotonic trends (0–12 h / 0–4 h)
#
# KEY CHANGE vs previous version:
#   Do NOT stop if some Osculum_ID are missing 0–30 or 30–60 min.
#   We:
#     (i) report missing-bin oscula
#     (ii) run the paired test on COMPLETE PAIRS ONLY
#
# Canonical input: df_12h_resp (already built earlier)
# Required: Respiration, rel_h, rel_min, rel_30, Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID
# Outputs:
#   - coverage table (CSV)
#   - paired Wilcoxon + summary (complete pairs only)
#   - Spearman per Osculum_ID (0–12 h and 0–4 h) + CSV
# ================================================================================================
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------
# 0) Checks + clean dataset
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("df_12h_resp"))
REQ <- c("Respiration","rel_h","rel_min","rel_30","Sponge_cod","Osculum_ID")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(df_12h_resp))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("df_12h_resp missing: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

df_12h_resp2 <- df_12h_resp %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(Respiration), is.finite(rel_h), is.finite(rel_30),
    !is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID)
  )

cat("Resp clean dataset (df_12h_resp2):\n")
cat("  rows:   ", nrow(df_12h_resp2), "\n")
cat("  sponges:", n_distinct(df_12h_resp2$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df_12h_resp2$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# ================================================================================================
# 1) Paired test (0–30 vs 30–60 min) within Osculum_ID
#    Run on COMPLETE PAIRS ONLY (do not stop if some are incomplete).
# ================================================================================================

coverage_0_60 <- df_12h_resp2 %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h < 1) %>%
  mutate(bin = if_else(rel_h < 0.5, "0_30", "30_60")) %>%
  count(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, bin, name = "n_points") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = bin, values_from = n_points, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(ok_pair = (`0_30` > 0) & (`30_60` > 0)) %>%
  arrange(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID)

cat("Coverage check (first hour):\n")
print(coverage_0_60 %>% count(ok_pair))

missing_pairs <- coverage_0_60 %>% filter(!ok_pair)
if (nrow(missing_pairs) > 0) {
  cat("\nOscula missing one of the bins (will be excluded from paired test):\n")
  print(missing_pairs, n = Inf)
}

# Save coverage table (recommended for supplement transparency)
write.csv(
  coverage_0_60,
  file.path(out_dir, "Resp_paired_coverage_0_30_vs_30_60min.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# Build paired dataset (means per bin per Osculum_ID)
paired_long_resp <- df_12h_resp2 %>%
  filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h < 1) %>%
  mutate(bin = if_else(rel_h < 0.5, "t0_0_30min", "t30_30_60min")) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, bin) %>%
  summarise(
    Resp_mean = mean(Respiration, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_points  = n(),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

paired_wide_resp <- paired_long_resp %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = c(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod),
    names_from  = bin,
    values_from = c(Resp_mean, n_points),
    values_fill = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Resp_mean_t0_0_30min), !is.na(Resp_mean_t30_30_60min)) %>%
  mutate(delta = Resp_mean_t30_30_60min - Resp_mean_t0_0_30min)

cat("\nPaired dataset (complete pairs only):\n")
cat("  pairs:", nrow(paired_wide_resp), "\n\n")

if (nrow(paired_wide_resp) >= 2) {
  wilc_res_resp <- wilcox.test(
    paired_wide_resp$Resp_mean_t0_0_30min,
    paired_wide_resp$Resp_mean_t30_30_60min,
    paired = TRUE,
    exact  = FALSE
  )
  cat("Wilcoxon paired test (Respiration, 0–30 vs 30–60 min):\n")
  print(wilc_res_resp)
} else {
  cat("Wilcoxon test skipped: <2 complete pairs.\n")
}

paired_summary_resp <- paired_wide_resp %>%
  summarise(
    n_pairs     = n(),
    median_t0   = median(Resp_mean_t0_0_30min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_t30  = median(Resp_mean_t30_30_60min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_diff   = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_diff     = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

cat("\nPaired summary (complete pairs only):\n")
print(paired_summary_resp)
cat("\n")

# Optional paired plot
p_paired_resp <- paired_wide_resp %>%
  select(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, Resp_mean_t0_0_30min, Resp_mean_t30_30_60min) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Resp_mean_t0_0_30min, Resp_mean_t30_30_60min),
    names_to = "time_bin",
    values_to = "Resp_mean"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    time_bin = factor(
      time_bin,
      levels = c("Resp_mean_t0_0_30min", "Resp_mean_t30_30_60min"),
      labels = c("0–30 min", "30–60 min")
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(time_bin, Resp_mean, group = Osculum_ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression("Respiration"~(mu*mol~O[2]~h^{-1})),
    title = "Paired comparison within Osculum_ID: 0–30 vs 30–60 min post-feeding (complete pairs)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
print(p_paired_resp)

# ================================================================================================
# 2) Spearman per Osculum_ID (Respiration vs rel_h) — 0–12 h and 0–4 h
# ================================================================================================
safe_spearman <- function(x, y, min_n = 4) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y) & !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
  x <- x[ok]; y <- y[ok]
  if (length(x) < min_n) return(list(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = length(x), ok = FALSE, reason = "n<min"))
  if (sd(x) == 0 || sd(y) == 0) return(list(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = length(x), ok = FALSE, reason = "sd=0"))
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  list(rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, n = length(x), ok = TRUE, reason = NA_character_)
}

spearman_by_osculum <- function(df, window_label, min_n = 4) {
  res <- df %>%
    group_by(Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod) %>%
    summarise(
      tmp = list(safe_spearman(Respiration, rel_h, min_n = min_n)),
      n   = tmp[[1]]$n,
      rho = tmp[[1]]$rho,
      p   = tmp[[1]]$p,
      ok  = tmp[[1]]$ok,
      reason = tmp[[1]]$reason,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    select(-tmp) %>%
    mutate(
      window = window_label,
      p_fdr  = ifelse(is.na(p), NA_real_, p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))
    )

  cat("Spearman diagnostics:", window_label, "\n")
  cat("  Total series:", nrow(res), "\n")
  cat("  Valid tests:", sum(res$ok, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("  Excluded (n<min):", sum(res$reason == "n<min", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("  Excluded (sd=0):", sum(res$reason == "sd=0", na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")
  res
}

sp_12h_resp <- spearman_by_osculum(df_12h_resp2 %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 12), "0–12 h")
sp_4h_resp  <- spearman_by_osculum(df_12h_resp2 %>% filter(rel_h >= 0, rel_h <= 4),  "0–4 h")
sp_all_resp <- bind_rows(sp_12h_resp, sp_4h_resp)

cat("Spearman per Osculum_ID summary (Respiration):\n")
print(
  sp_all_resp %>%
    group_by(window) %>%
    summarise(
      n_series    = n(),
      n_valid     = sum(!is.na(rho)),
      n_sig_p05   = sum(p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_sig_fdr05 = sum(p_fdr < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_rho  = median(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_rho    = mean(rho, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups     = "drop"
    )
)

write.csv(
  sp_all_resp %>% select(window, Osculum_ID, Sponge_cod, n, ok, reason, rho, p, p_fdr),
  file.path(out_dir, "Resp_spearman_by_OsculumID_0_12h_and_0_4h.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

# Plots
ggplot(sp_all_resp %>% filter(!is.na(rho)), aes(rho)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Spearman rho (Respiration vs time since feeding)",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of per-osculum Spearman correlations (Respiration)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(sp_all_resp %>% filter(!is.na(rho)), aes(Sponge_cod, rho)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0)) +
  facet_wrap(~ window, ncol = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Sponge",
    y = "Spearman rho",
    title = "Per-osculum rho values by sponge (Respiration)"
  ) +
  theme_classic()




# rho values used for the reported correlations (0–12 h)
rho_12 <- sp_12h_resp %>% filter(ok) %>% pull(rho)

# XX = mean rho
XX <- mean(rho_12)

# YY = 95% CI half-width for the mean rho (bootstrap, same style as before)
set.seed(1)
boot_means <- replicate(2000, mean(sample(rho_12, replace = TRUE)))
ci <- quantile(boot_means, c(0.025, 0.975), names = FALSE)
YY <- (ci[2] - ci[1]) / 2

# range check (should match your -0.71 to 0.53 if that’s correct)
range_12 <- range(rho_12)

XX; YY; range_12

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################


```

### Synchrony in respiration between paired sponges: Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot)

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

################################################################################
# Synchrony in respiration between paired sponges
# Spearman correlation computed WITHIN each run, summarized by pair (boxplot)
#
# INPUT:
#   df_base must include: Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Sponge_cod, Respiration
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   1) df_pairwide_resp       : paired-wide table (A/B) per timestamp and run
#   2) cor_pair_by_run_resp   : Pearson & Spearman per Pair_ID × Repeat_Index
#   3) p_resp_box             : boxplot of Spearman rho distributions per Pair_ID
#   4) summary_RESP           : mean/median rho per pair + Overall + ±CI95
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# USER SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
min_nRESP <- 9  # minimum paired timestamps per Pair_ID × Repeat_Index to compute correlation

pair_colors <- c(
  "82-83" = "#FF69B4",
  "84-85" = "#7A378B",
  "86-87" = "#104E8B"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1 — Clean + keep only finite Respiration rows (minimal types)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_resp <- df_base %>%
  mutate(
    Repeat_Index  = as.integer(Repeat_Index),
    Sponge_cod    = as.character(Sponge_cod),
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Respiration   = as.numeric(Respiration)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Respiration))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2 — Keep only columns needed for pairing
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_keep_resp <- df_resp %>%
  select(Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Sponge_cod, Respiration) %>%
  arrange(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Sponge_cod)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3 — Keep only true parallel measurements (exactly two sponges)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_keep2_resp <- df_keep_resp %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Sponge_cod) == 2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Quick sanity check: should only have 2 sponges per timepoint
sanity_nsp_resp <- df_keep2_resp %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  summarise(n_sponges = n_distinct(Sponge_cod), .groups = "drop") %>%
  count(n_sponges)

print(sanity_nsp_resp)
stopifnot(all(sanity_nsp_resp$n_sponges == 2))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4 — Build paired-wide table (A/B)
#   A = smaller Sponge_cod, B = larger Sponge_cod (stable biological definition)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_pairwide_resp <- df_keep2_resp %>%
  group_by(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time) %>%
  summarise(
    A_id    = min(Sponge_cod),
    B_id    = max(Sponge_cod),
    Pair_ID = paste(A_id, B_id, sep = "-"),
    A       = Respiration[Sponge_cod == A_id][1],
    B       = Respiration[Sponge_cod == B_id][1],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  select(Date_and_time, Repeat_Index, Pair_ID, A, B) %>%
  arrange(Repeat_Index, Date_and_time, Pair_ID)

# QC: ensure no missing A/B
stopifnot(all(is.finite(df_pairwide_resp$A)), all(is.finite(df_pairwide_resp$B)))

# QC: pairs per run (expect 3 if design fixed)
pairs_per_run_resp <- df_pairwide_resp %>%
  distinct(Repeat_Index, Pair_ID) %>%
  count(Repeat_Index, name = "n_pairs")
print(pairs_per_run_resp)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5 — Correlation WITHIN each run (Pair_ID × Repeat_Index)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cor_pair_by_run_resp <- df_pairwide_resp %>%
  group_by(Pair_ID, Repeat_Index) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points   = n(),
    pearson_r  = if (n_points >= min_nRESP) cor(A, B, method = "pearson",  use = "complete.obs") else NA_real_,
    spearman_r = if (n_points >= min_nRESP) cor(A, B, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs") else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(spearman_r))

# Quick check: number of runs contributing per pair
runs_per_pair_resp <- cor_pair_by_run_resp %>%
  count(Pair_ID, name = "n_runs_with_corr")
print(runs_per_pair_resp)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 6 — Summary table: per pair + Overall
#   mean / median Spearman rho + 95% bootstrap CI half-width (±CI)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
bootstrap_ci_halfwidth <- function(x, conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 3) return(NA_real_)

  boot_means <- replicate(nboot, mean(sample(x, replace = TRUE)))

  alpha <- (1 - conf) / 2
  ci <- quantile(boot_means, probs = c(alpha, 1 - alpha), names = FALSE)

  (ci[2] - ci[1]) / 2
}

summarise_cor_table <- function(cor_by_run, r_col = "spearman_r",
                                conf = 0.95, nboot = 2000) {

  stopifnot(all(c("Pair_ID", "Repeat_Index", r_col) %in% names(cor_by_run)))

  per_pair <- cor_by_run %>%
    group_by(Pair_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(Pair_ID)

  overall <- cor_by_run %>%
    summarise(
      Pair_ID  = "Overall",
      n_runs   = n(),
      mean_r   = mean(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      median_r = median(.data[[r_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      ci_95    = bootstrap_ci_halfwidth(.data[[r_col]], conf, nboot)
    )

  bind_rows(per_pair, overall)
}

summary_RESP <- summarise_cor_table(cor_pair_by_run_resp, r_col = "spearman_r")
summary_RESP

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 7 — Publication-style boxplot (white background, no grid)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_resp_box <- ggplot(cor_pair_by_run_resp, aes(x = Pair_ID, y = spearman_r, fill = Pair_ID)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    width = 0.6,
    outlier.shape = 21,
    outlier.size = 2,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.12,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pair_colors) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 15) +
  labs(
    title    = "Within-run synchrony in respiration",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "Sponge pair",
    y = expression("Spearman correlation (" * rho * ")")
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13)
  )

p_resp_box

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 8 — Save figure
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Spearman_boxplot_pairs_Respiration.png",
  plot     = p_resp_box,
  path     = "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R",
  width    = 7,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white"
)




```



## ΔO2 vs OFR

### ΔO2 vs OFR — Instantaneous (minute-scale) relationship (canonical pipeline)
```{r, fig.width=10,fig.height=7}

####################################################################################################
# ΔO2 vs OFR — Instantaneous (minute-scale) relationship (canonical pipeline)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GOAL
#   Test whether oxygen removal (ΔO2; In–Ex concentration difference) is associated with osculum
#   flow rate (OFR) at the raw, high-frequency measurement scale (no temporal averaging).
#
# WHY MIXED MODELS
#   Observations are nested / repeated:
#     measurement -> Osculum_ID (Sponge_cod × Repeat_Code) -> Sponge_cod
#   Mixed models account for non-independence via random effects.
#
# CANONICAL INPUT
#   - iv_feeding_base must exist (built earlier once in the new workflow)
#   - Required columns: OFR, ΔO2, Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID
#
# OUTPUT
#   - 2 plots (pooled + faceted)
#   - LMMs: m1 (RI), m2 (RI + random slope), m_wb (within-between), optional m_log
#   - Saved figures in Chapter 2 folder
####################################################################################################


####################################################################################################
# Build canonical Osculum_ID inside iv_feeding_base (if missing)
# Definition:
#   Osculum_ID = Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code   (e.g., 82_1_A)
####################################################################################################

stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))
stopifnot(all(c("Sponge_cod","Repeat_Code") %in% names(iv_feeding_base)))

if (!("Osculum_ID" %in% names(iv_feeding_base))) {
  iv_feeding_base <- iv_feeding_base %>%
    mutate(
      Sponge_cod  = as.integer(Sponge_cod),
      Repeat_Code = as.character(Repeat_Code),
      Osculum_ID  = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_"))
    )
}

# quick sanity
cat("Osculum_ID exists:", "Osculum_ID" %in% names(iv_feeding_base), "\n")
cat("n oscula:", nlevels(iv_feeding_base$Osculum_ID), "\n")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)

# -------------------------
# 0) Checks + palette
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))
REQ <- c("OFR","ΔO2","Sponge_cod","Osculum_ID")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("iv_feeding_base missing: ", paste(miss, collapse=", "))

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R/Figures"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# -------------------------
# 1) Clean analysis dataframe
# -------------------------
df_do2_ofr <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  transmute(
    Sponge_cod = factor(Sponge_cod, levels = names(cols)),
    Osculum_ID = factor(Osculum_ID),
    OFR        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR)),
    DO2        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR), is.finite(DO2)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Sponge_cod), !is.na(Osculum_ID))

cat("Instantaneous dataset (df_do2_ofr)\n")
cat("  n rows:  ", nrow(df_do2_ofr), "\n")
cat("  sponges: ", n_distinct(df_do2_ofr$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  oscula:  ", n_distinct(df_do2_ofr$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("  OFR<=0:  ", sum(df_do2_ofr$OFR <= 0), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 2) Visualization
# -------------------------
# A) pooled + global linear trend (visual only)
p_all <- ggplot(df_do2_ofr, aes(OFR, DO2, color = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.4) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 1.0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = expression("OFR (L "*h^{-1}*")"),
    y = expression(Delta*O[2]~"(µmol O"[2]~L^{-1}*")"),
    color = "Sponge ID"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"))

p_all

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_vs_OFR_instantaneous_pooled.png"),
  plot     = p_all,
  width    = 18, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 600
)

# B) faceted per sponge
p_facet <- ggplot(df_do2_ofr, aes(OFR, DO2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sponge_cod), alpha = 0.35, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = expression("OFR (L "*h^{-1}*")"),
    y = expression(Delta*O[2]~"(µmol O"[2]~L^{-1}*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.border     = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black")
  )

p_facet

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_vs_OFR_instantaneous_by_sponge.png"),
  plot     = p_facet,
  width    = 18, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 600
)

# Optional zoom (keeps all points, just changes view window)
p_all + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 25))
p_facet + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 25))

# -------------------------
# 3) Mixed models (inference)
# -------------------------
# m1: random intercepts (sponge + osculum)
m1 <- lmer(DO2 ~ OFR + (1 | Sponge_cod) + (1 | Osculum_ID),
           data = df_do2_ofr, REML = TRUE)
summary(m1)

# m2: random slope of OFR per osculum (tests heterogeneous OFR–ΔO2 slopes)
m2 <- lmer(DO2 ~ OFR + (1 + OFR | Osculum_ID) + (1 | Sponge_cod),
           data = df_do2_ofr, REML = TRUE,
           control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
summary(m2)
anova(m1, m2)   # LRT via ML refit (done internally)

# Within–between decomposition (recommended interpretation)
df_wb <- df_do2_ofr %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  mutate(
    OFR_mean_os = mean(OFR, na.rm = TRUE),
    OFR_within  = OFR - OFR_mean_os
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

m_wb <- lmer(DO2 ~ OFR_within + OFR_mean_os + (1 | Sponge_cod) + (1 | Osculum_ID),
             data = df_wb, REML = TRUE)
summary(m_wb)

# Optional sensitivity: log10(OFR + eps) (handle right-skew / wide scale)
df_wb <- df_wb %>% mutate(logOFR = log10(OFR + 0.01))
m_log <- lmer(DO2 ~ logOFR + (1 | Sponge_cod) + (1 | Osculum_ID),
              data = df_wb, REML = TRUE)
summary(m_log)

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# 1) הגרף הראשי (כמו אצלך)
p_all <- ggplot(df_do2_ofr, aes(OFR, DO2, color = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.4) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 1.0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = expression("OFR (L "*h^{-1}*")"),
    y = expression(Delta*O[2]~"(µmol O"[2]~L^{-1}*")"),
    color = "Sponge ID"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"))

# 2) Inset: אותו גרף אבל עם zoom
p_zoom <- p_all +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 25)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6)
  )

# 3) מסמנים על הגרף הראשי את האזור שמוגדל
p_main_marked <- p_all +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 0, xmax = 5,
           ymin = 0, ymax = 25,
           fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6)

# 4) מדביקים inset על הגרף הראשי (x,y,width,height הם ביחידות יחסיות 0..1)
p_inset <- p_main_marked +
  inset_element(
    p_zoom,
    left = 0.35,
    bottom = 0.38,   # היה 0.08
    right = 0.98,
    top = 0.98,      # היה 0.48
    align_to = "panel"
  )

p_inset


ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_DO2_vs_OFR_instantaneous_with_inset.png",
  plot     = p_inset,
  path     = "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R/Figures",
  width    = 18,
  height   = 14,
  units    = "cm",
  dpi      = 600,
  bg       = "white"
)


p_inset




```



### ΔO2 vs OFR — 3-hour binning + linear (mixed) models (robust to missing Osculum_ID)
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# ΔO2 vs OFR — 3-hour binning + LMs/LMMs (canonical pipeline, robust IDs)
# Fix:
#   Build Osculum_ID if missing (Sponge_cod + Repeat_Code / Repeat_Index)
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(lubridate)

# -------------------------
# 0) Checks + palette
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))

REQ0 <- c("Date_and_time","OFR","ΔO2","Sponge_cod")
miss0 <- setdiff(REQ0, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss0) > 0) stop("iv_feeding_base missing: ", paste(miss0, collapse=", "))

repeat_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Repeat_Code"  %in% names(iv_feeding_base) ~ "Repeat_Code",
  "Repeat_Index" %in% names(iv_feeding_base) ~ "Repeat_Index",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(repeat_col)) {
  stop("iv_feeding_base is missing Repeat_Code / Repeat_Index (needed to build Osculum_ID).")
}

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R/Figures"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

if (!exists("cols")) {
  cols <- c(
    "82" = "#FF69B4",
    "83" = "#8B3A62",
    "84" = "#E066FF",
    "85" = "#7A378B",
    "86" = "#1C86EE",
    "87" = "#104E8B"
  )
}

# -------------------------
# 1) Clean raw dataframe + ensure Osculum_ID
# -------------------------
df <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  mutate(
    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Sponge_cod    = factor(Sponge_cod, levels = names(cols)),
    Repeat_tmp    = .data[[repeat_col]],
    Osculum_ID    = if ("Osculum_ID" %in% names(iv_feeding_base)) {
      as.character(Osculum_ID)
    } else {
      paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_tmp, sep = "_")
    }
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Sponge_cod    = Sponge_cod,
    Osculum_ID    = factor(Osculum_ID),
    Date_and_time = Date_and_time,
    OFR           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR)),
    DO2           = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR), is.finite(DO2)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Date_and_time), !is.na(Sponge_cod), !is.na(Osculum_ID))

cat("Raw (filtered) rows:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Sponges:", n_distinct(df$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("Oscula: ", n_distinct(df$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 2) 3-hour bins
# -------------------------
df_3h <- df %>%
  mutate(bin_3h = floor_date(Date_and_time, unit = "3 hours")) %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID, bin_3h) %>%
  summarise(
    OFR_mean = mean(OFR, na.rm = TRUE),
    DO2_mean = mean(DO2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_bin    = n(),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_bin >= 5)

cat("3h-binned rows:", nrow(df_3h), "\n")
cat("Median n_bin:", median(df_3h$n_bin), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 3) Plots
# -------------------------
p3h_all <- ggplot(df_3h, aes(OFR_mean, DO2_mean, color = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 2.0) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 1.0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Mean OFR (3 h bins, L "*h^{-1}*")"),
    y = expression("Mean "~Delta*O[2]~"(3 h bins, µmol O"[2]~L^{-1}*")"),
    color = "Sponge ID"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"))

p3h_all

p3h_facet <- ggplot(df_3h, aes(OFR_mean, DO2_mean)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Sponge_cod), alpha = 0.70, size = 2.0) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Mean OFR (3 h bins, L "*h^{-1}*")"),
    y = expression("Mean "~Delta*O[2]~"(3 h bins, µmol O"[2]~L^{-1}*")")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.border     = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black")
  )

p3h_facet

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_DO2_vs_OFR_3h_binned_by_sponge.png"),
  plot     = p3h_facet,
  width    = 14, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300
)

# -------------------------
# 4) Models on binned means
# -------------------------
lm_3h <- lm(DO2_mean ~ OFR_mean, data = df_3h)
summary(lm_3h)

m3h_lmm <- lmer(DO2_mean ~ OFR_mean + (1 | Osculum_ID) + (1 | Sponge_cod),
                data = df_3h, REML = TRUE)
summary(m3h_lmm)

df_3h_wb <- df_3h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  mutate(
    OFR_mean_os = mean(OFR_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    OFR_within  = OFR_mean - OFR_mean_os
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

m3h_wb <- lmer(DO2_mean ~ OFR_within + OFR_mean_os + (1 | Osculum_ID) + (1 | Sponge_cod),
               data = df_3h_wb, REML = TRUE)
summary(m3h_wb)

m3h_lmm_w <- lmer(DO2_mean ~ OFR_mean + (1 | Osculum_ID) + (1 | Sponge_cod),
                  data = df_3h, weights = n_bin, REML = TRUE)
summary(m3h_lmm_w)

####################################################################################################
# End
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# -------------------------
# Helper: CV function
# -------------------------
cv <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 2 || mean(x) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  sd(x) / abs(mean(x))
}

# =========================
# 1) CV BEFORE binning (raw, minute-scale)
# =========================
cv_raw <- df %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    CV_raw = cv(DO2),
    n_raw  = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# =========================
# 2) CV AFTER binning (3 h means)
# =========================
cv_3h <- df_3h %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    CV_3h = cv(DO2_mean),
    n_3h  = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# =========================
# 3) Combine + summary
# =========================
cv_compare <- cv_raw %>%
  inner_join(cv_3h, by = "Osculum_ID")

summary_cv <- cv_compare %>%
  summarise(
    n_oscula = n(),
    median_CV_raw = median(CV_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_CV_raw    = IQR(CV_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_CV_3h  = median(CV_3h, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_CV_3h     = IQR(CV_3h, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

summary_cv



```

### Distribution of per-osculum Spearman correlations ρ(OFR, ΔO2)

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# Fig. 3.2.8 — Distribution of per-osculum Spearman correlations ρ(OFR, ΔO2)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLE (Results)
#   Quantifies heterogeneity among oscula by computing Spearman rank correlations (ρ) between OFR
#   and ΔO2 within each Osculum_ID. This addresses whether a null pooled relationship could conceal
#   opposite monotonic trends in different oscula.
#
# ANALYSIS UNIT
#   Osculum_ID = osculum-level time series (Sponge_cod × Repeat_Code).
#
# METHOD (as described in Methods)
#   - Compute ρ within each Osculum_ID, requiring:
#       (i) minimum temporal coverage (min_n points)
#      (ii) within-series variability (sd>0 in both variables)
#   - Adjust p-values across oscula using BH-FDR.
#
# OUTPUT
#   - Faceted histogram by sponge (main figure) + optional overall histogram
#   - Wilcoxon signed-rank test vs median ρ = 0 (optional; matches Results text)
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------
# 0) Checks + palette + paths
# -------------------------
stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))
REQ <- c("Sponge_cod","Repeat_Code","OFR","ΔO2")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("iv_feeding_base missing: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

cols <- c(
  "82"="#FF69B4","83"="#8B3A62","84"="#E066FF",
  "85"="#7A378B","86"="#1C86EE","87"="#104E8B"
)

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R/Figures"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# 1) Clean dataset (minute-scale OFR + ΔO2)
#    Note: we construct Osculum_ID here to keep the chunk self-contained
# -------------------------
df_DO2_OFR <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod  = factor(as.integer(Sponge_cod), levels = names(cols)),
    Repeat_Code = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID  = factor(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_")),
    OFR = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR)),
    DO2 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR), is.finite(DO2), !is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Sponge_cod))

cat("Per-osculum Spearman ρ(OFR, ΔO2)\n")
cat("  n obs:   ", nrow(df_DO2_OFR), "\n")
cat("  n sponges:", n_distinct(df_DO2_OFR$Sponge_cod), "\n")
cat("  n oscula: ", n_distinct(df_DO2_OFR$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 2) Safe Spearman per group
# -------------------------
spearman_one_group <- function(x, y, min_n = 30) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
  x <- x[ok]; y <- y[ok]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < min_n) return(c(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = n))
  if (sd(x) == 0 || sd(y) == 0) return(c(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = n))
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  c(rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, n = n)
}

# -------------------------
# 3) Compute rho table (one row per Osculum_ID)
# -------------------------
rho_tbl <- df_DO2_OFR %>%
  group_by(Sponge_cod, Osculum_ID) %>%
  summarise(out = list(spearman_one_group(OFR, DO2, min_n = 30)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  unnest_wider(out) %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))

cat("Valid correlations:", sum(!is.na(rho_tbl$rho)), " / ", nrow(rho_tbl), "\n")
cat("BH-FDR sig (p_adj<0.05):", sum(rho_tbl$p_adj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 4) Main plot: faceted by sponge (matches Fig. 3.2.8)
# -------------------------
p_rho_by_sponge <- ggplot(rho_tbl %>% filter(!is.na(rho)),
                          aes(x = rho, fill = Sponge_cod)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.0) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sponge_cod, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  labs(
    x = expression("Spearman "~rho~" (per osculum): OFR vs "~Delta*O[2]),
    y = "Number of oscula"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_rho_by_sponge

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_328_Spearman_rho_OFR_vs_DO2_by_sponge.png"),
  plot   = p_rho_by_sponge,
  width  = 14, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

# -------------------------
# 5) Optional: is the median rho different from 0?
# -------------------------
rho_vec <- rho_tbl$rho[is.finite(rho_tbl$rho)]
wilcox.test(rho_vec, mu = 0)



```


### Spearman correlation heatmap across osculum-level mean values

```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}
####################################################################################################
# Fig. 3.2.9 (SAME DESIGN) — Spearman correlation heatmap with EVENT-LINKED metabolic means
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GOAL (Reviewer):
#   Replace "global osculum means" with metabolic means aligned to each TOC/FCM sampling event.
#
# WINDOW RULE (in MINUTES):
#   - For "before" samples: mean OFR/ΔO2/Respiration over win_min minutes BEFORE the event time
#   - For "after"  samples: mean OFR/ΔO2/Respiration over win_min minutes AFTER  the event time
#
# IMPORTANT:
#   Plot design stays identical. Only the input table to the correlation is rebuilt.
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(grid)
library(lubridate)

stopifnot(exists("iv_feeding_base"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# USER SETTINGS  (ONLY CHANGE HERE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_2_F&R"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

cell_groups <- c("Bact", "Syn", "Euk_Large", "Euk_Small")

# Window length in MINUTES (easy to change: 60, 90, 120, ...)
win_min <- 90

# Feeding labels expected in your data
valid_feeding <- c("before", "after")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) REQUIRED COLUMNS CHECK
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REQ <- c("Sponge_cod","Repeat_Code","Date_and_time","Feeding",
         "OFR","ΔO2","Respiration","TOC","Groups","Cell.concentration")
miss <- setdiff(REQ, names(iv_feeding_base))
if (length(miss) > 0) stop("iv_feeding_base missing: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) CLEAN + STANDARDIZE COLUMNS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df0 <- iv_feeding_base %>%
  mutate(
    Sponge_cod  = factor(as.integer(Sponge_cod)),
    Repeat_Code = as.character(Repeat_Code),
    Osculum_ID  = as.character(paste(Sponge_cod, Repeat_Code, sep = "_")),

    Date_and_time = as.POSIXct(Date_and_time),
    Feeding       = as.character(Feeding),
    Groups        = as.character(Groups),

    TOC                = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(TOC)),
    Cell.concentration = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Cell.concentration)),
    OFR                = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(OFR)),
    Respiration        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Respiration)),
    DO2                = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`ΔO2`))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Osculum_ID), !is.na(Date_and_time))

cat("Heatmap input\n")
cat("  raw rows:", nrow(df0), "\n")
cat("  oscula: ", n_distinct(df0$Osculum_ID), "\n")
cat("  sponges:", n_distinct(df0$Sponge_cod), "\n\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) DEFINE "SAMPLING EVENTS" (TOC or FCM) PER OSCULUM
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Event = timepoint where Feeding is before/after AND there is TOC OR FCM (cell group value)
events <- df0 %>%
  filter(Feeding %in% valid_feeding) %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(TOC) |
      (Groups %in% cell_groups & is.finite(Cell.concentration))
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    Osculum_ID,
    event_time = Date_and_time,
    Feeding,
    TOC_event  = TOC,
    Groups,
    Cell_event = Cell.concentration
  )

cat("Sampling events\n")
cat("  rows (events):", nrow(events), "\n")
cat("  oscula with events:", n_distinct(events$Osculum_ID), "\n\n")

# Optional diagnostics: how many before/after rows exist overall in df0
cat("Feeding counts in df0:\n")
print(table(df0$Feeding, useNA = "ifany"))
cat("\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) ASSIGN TIME WINDOW AROUND EACH EVENT (IN MINUTES)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Before: [event_time - win_min, event_time]
# After : [event_time,           event_time + win_min]
events <- events %>%
  mutate(
    t_start = if_else(Feeding == "before", event_time - minutes(win_min), event_time),
    t_end   = if_else(Feeding == "before", event_time,                   event_time + minutes(win_min))
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3b) QUICK WINDOW COVERAGE CHECK (first 5 events)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ts_small <- df0 %>% select(Osculum_ID, Date_and_time, OFR, DO2, Respiration)

check5 <- events %>%
  distinct(Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding, t_start, t_end) %>%
  slice(1:5) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    n_in_window = sum(
      ts_small$Osculum_ID == Osculum_ID &
        ts_small$Date_and_time >= t_start &
        ts_small$Date_and_time <= t_end
    ),
    n_OFR = sum(
      ts_small$Osculum_ID == Osculum_ID &
        ts_small$Date_and_time >= t_start &
        ts_small$Date_and_time <= t_end &
        is.finite(ts_small$OFR)
    ),
    n_DO2 = sum(
      ts_small$Osculum_ID == Osculum_ID &
        ts_small$Date_and_time >= t_start &
        ts_small$Date_and_time <= t_end &
        is.finite(ts_small$DO2)
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding, t_start, t_end, n_in_window, n_OFR, n_DO2)

cat("Window coverage check (first 5 events):\n")
print(check5)
cat("\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) COMPUTE WINDOWED MEANS OF METABOLIC VARIABLES PER EVENT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Strategy:
#   - Join each event to the time-series of its Osculum_ID
#   - Keep only timepoints within [t_start, t_end]
#   - Average OFR / DO2 / Respiration within that window
event_met <- events %>%
  left_join(ts_small, by = "Osculum_ID") %>%
  filter(Date_and_time >= t_start, Date_and_time <= t_end) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding, TOC_event, Groups, Cell_event) %>%
  summarise(
    `Mean(OFR)`         = mean(OFR, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Mean(Respiration)` = mean(Respiration, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Mean(ΔO2)`         = mean(DO2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_points_window     = dplyr::n(),
    n_OFR_points        = sum(is.finite(OFR)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("Event-linked metabolic means\n")
cat("  rows:", nrow(event_met), "\n")
cat("  median window points:", median(event_met$n_points_window, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  median OFR points per event-window:", median(event_met$n_OFR_points, na.rm = TRUE), "\n\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) BUILD THE FINAL CORRELATION TABLE (same column layout as original)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE:
#   - TOC_event and Cell_event are EVENT values (not window-averaged)
#   - Mean(OFR)/Mean(ΔO2)/Mean(Respiration) are window-averaged
toc_wide <- event_met %>%
  transmute(
    Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding,
    `Mean(TOC)` = TOC_event
  ) %>%
  distinct()

cell_wide <- event_met %>%
  filter(Groups %in% cell_groups) %>%
  transmute(
    Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding,
    colname = paste0("Mean(Cell concentration, ", Groups, ")"),
    value   = Cell_event
  ) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = colname, values_from = value)

met_wide <- event_met %>%
  transmute(
    Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding,
    `Mean(OFR)`,
    `Mean(Respiration)`,
    `Mean(ΔO2)`
  ) %>%
  distinct()

corr_df <- met_wide %>%
  left_join(toc_wide,  by = c("Osculum_ID","event_time","Feeding")) %>%
  left_join(cell_wide, by = c("Osculum_ID","event_time","Feeding"))

vars_for_cor <- c(
  paste0("Mean(Cell concentration, ", cell_groups, ")"),
  "Mean(TOC)",
  "Mean(OFR)",
  "Mean(Respiration)",
  "Mean(ΔO2)"
)

corr_mat_df <- corr_df %>% select(any_of(vars_for_cor))

cat("Event-level table used for correlations\n")
cat("  n events (rows):", nrow(corr_mat_df), "\n")
cat("  columns:", ncol(corr_mat_df), "\n")
print(colSums(is.finite(as.matrix(corr_mat_df))))
cat("\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5b) OPTIONAL: overlap diagnostics at EVENT level (TOC + cell + OFR)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
event_flags <- event_met %>%
  mutate(
    has_TOC  = is.finite(TOC_event),
    has_cell = Groups %in% cell_groups & is.finite(Cell_event),
    has_OFR  = is.finite(`Mean(OFR)`)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Osculum_ID, event_time, Feeding) %>%
  summarise(
    has_TOC  = any(has_TOC),
    has_cell = any(has_cell),
    has_OFR  = any(has_OFR),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("Overlap diagnostics (EVENT unique):\n")
print(
  event_flags %>%
    summarise(
      n_events_total = n(),
      n_all_three    = sum(has_TOC & has_cell & has_OFR),
      n_TOC_OFR      = sum(has_TOC & has_OFR & !has_cell),
      n_cells_OFR    = sum(has_cell & has_OFR & !has_TOC),
      n_TOC_cells    = sum(has_TOC & has_cell & !has_OFR)
    )
)
cat("\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) SPEARMAN CORRELATION MATRIX (same as original)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
corr_mat <- cor(
  corr_mat_df,
  method = "spearman",
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)

corr_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(corr_mat)) %>%
  rename(rho = Freq) %>%
  mutate(
    Var1 = factor(Var1, levels = vars_for_cor),
    Var2 = factor(Var2, levels = vars_for_cor)
  )

axis_labels <- c(
  "Mean(Cell concentration, Bact)"      = "Bact",
  "Mean(Cell concentration, Syn)"       = "Syn",
  "Mean(Cell concentration, Euk_Large)" = "Euk_Large",
  "Mean(Cell concentration, Euk_Small)" = "Euk_Small",
  "Mean(TOC)"                           = "TOC",
  "Mean(OFR)"                           = "OFR",
  "Mean(Respiration)"                   = "Respiration",
  "Mean(ΔO2)"                           = expression(Delta*O[2])
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) UPPER TRIANGLE ONLY (same as original)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
corr_long2 <- corr_long %>%
  mutate(i = as.integer(Var1),
         j = as.integer(Var2)) %>%
  filter(i <= j)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) DIAGONAL MINI-DENSITIES (identical styling)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
mini_density_grob <- function(x, line_col = "white", med_col = "white") {
  d <- data.frame(x = x)
  p <- ggplot(d, aes(x)) +
    geom_density(linewidth = 0.7, color = line_col, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
               linewidth = 0.5, color = med_col, linetype = 2) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA)
    )
  ggplotGrob(p)
}

diag_grobs <- lapply(vars_for_cor, function(v) {
  mini_density_grob(corr_mat_df[[v]], line_col = "white", med_col = "white")
})
names(diag_grobs) <- vars_for_cor

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) PLOT HEATMAP (IDENTICAL DESIGN)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_base <- ggplot(corr_long2, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#3B4CC0", mid = "white", high = "#B40426",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "Spearman ρ"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = axis_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = axis_labels) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

p_final <- p_base
for (idx in seq_along(vars_for_cor)) {
  v <- vars_for_cor[idx]
  p_final <- p_final +
    annotation_custom(
      grob = diag_grobs[[v]],
      xmin = idx - 0.5, xmax = idx + 0.5,
      ymin = idx - 0.5, ymax = idx + 0.5
    )
}

p_final

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10) SAVE FIGURE  (filename includes minutes so you never get confused)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir,
                       paste0("Fig_329_corr_heatmap_Spearman_EVENT_LINKED_",
                              win_min, "minWindow_triangle_diagDensity.png")),
  plot = p_final,
  width = 12, height = 9, units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

```


```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

####################################################################################################
# Spurious-correlation check for product-derived Respiration (osculum-level means)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROLE (Methods + Results support)
#   Respiration is defined as a product:  R = OFR × ΔO2.
#   Therefore, correlations of R with OFR or ΔO2 can arise even if OFR and ΔO2 are biologically
#   independent ("product-induced" / spurious association).
#
# ANALYSIS UNIT
#   Osculum_ID (one row per osculum series = Sponge_cod × Repeat_Code), using osculum-level means
#   generated in Fig. 3.2.9 (corr_df).
#
# GOALS
#   1) Report variability summaries across oscula (CV + robust CV).
#   2) Compute observed Spearman correlations: cor(OFR, R), cor(ΔO2, R).
#   3) Generate null distributions via permutation that decouple OFR and ΔO2 across oscula while
#      preserving each marginal distribution:
#        A) Null for cor(OFR, R): shuffle ΔO2 across oscula; R* = OFR × shuffled(ΔO2).
#        B) Null for cor(ΔO2, R): shuffle OFR across oscula; R* = shuffled(OFR) × ΔO2.
#   4) Compare observed |ρ| to null |ρ| (two-sided empirical p-value).
#
# REQUIRED OBJECTS (from Fig. 3.2.9 chunk)
#   - corr_df with columns: Mean(OFR), Mean(ΔO2), Mean(Respiration)
#   - out_dir exists
####################################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("corr_df"))

# -------------------------
# 1) Extract osculum-level means (complete cases)
# -------------------------
dat_os <- corr_df %>%
  transmute(
    Osculum_ID = Osculum_ID,
    OFR = `Mean(OFR)`,
    DO2 = `Mean(ΔO2)`,
    R   = `Mean(Respiration)`
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(OFR), is.finite(DO2), is.finite(R))

n_os <- nrow(dat_os)
cat("Spurious-corr check\n")
cat("  oscula used (complete cases):", n_os, "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 2) Variability summaries
# -------------------------
cv  <- function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
rcv <- function(x) mad(x, constant = 1, na.rm = TRUE) / median(x, na.rm = TRUE)

cv_table <- tibble(
  var  = c("OFR", "ΔO2", "Respiration"),
  mean = c(mean(dat_os$OFR), mean(dat_os$DO2), mean(dat_os$R)),
  sd   = c(sd(dat_os$OFR),   sd(dat_os$DO2),   sd(dat_os$R)),
  CV   = c(cv(dat_os$OFR),   cv(dat_os$DO2),   cv(dat_os$R)),
  rCV  = c(rcv(dat_os$OFR),  rcv(dat_os$DO2),  rcv(dat_os$R))
)
print(cv_table)
cat("\n")

# -------------------------
# 3) Observed Spearman correlations
# -------------------------
obs_cor_OFR <- suppressWarnings(cor(dat_os$OFR, dat_os$R, method = "spearman"))
obs_cor_DO2 <- suppressWarnings(cor(dat_os$DO2, dat_os$R, method = "spearman"))

cat("Observed Spearman correlations\n")
cat("  rho(OFR, R):  ", round(obs_cor_OFR, 3), "\n")
cat("  rho(ΔO2, R):  ", round(obs_cor_DO2, 3), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 4) Permutation null distributions (component-specific)
# -------------------------
set.seed(1)
B <- 5000

null_cor_OFR <- numeric(B)
null_cor_DO2 <- numeric(B)

for (b in seq_len(B)) {
  # A) Null for rho(OFR, R): shuffle ΔO2
  DO2_shuf <- sample(dat_os$DO2, size = n_os, replace = FALSE)
  R_star_A <- dat_os$OFR * DO2_shuf
  null_cor_OFR[b] <- suppressWarnings(cor(dat_os$OFR, R_star_A, method = "spearman"))

  # B) Null for rho(ΔO2, R): shuffle OFR
  OFR_shuf <- sample(dat_os$OFR, size = n_os, replace = FALSE)
  R_star_B <- OFR_shuf * dat_os$DO2
  null_cor_DO2[b] <- suppressWarnings(cor(dat_os$DO2, R_star_B, method = "spearman"))
}

cat("Null summaries\n")
cat("  mean null rho(OFR, R*): ", round(mean(null_cor_OFR), 3), "\n")
cat("  mean null rho(ΔO2, R*): ", round(mean(null_cor_DO2), 3), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 5) Empirical p-values (two-sided, based on |rho|)
# -------------------------
p_emp_OFR_2s <- mean(abs(null_cor_OFR) >= abs(obs_cor_OFR))
p_emp_DO2_2s <- mean(abs(null_cor_DO2) >= abs(obs_cor_DO2))

cat("Empirical p-values (two-sided, |rho_null| >= |rho_obs|)\n")
cat("  p for rho(OFR, R): ", signif(p_emp_OFR_2s, 3), "\n")
cat("  p for rho(ΔO2, R): ", signif(p_emp_DO2_2s, 3), "\n\n")

# -------------------------
# 6) Plot + save
# -------------------------
df_null <- bind_rows(
  tibble(rho = null_cor_OFR, type = "Null: rho(OFR, R*)"),
  tibble(rho = null_cor_DO2, type = "Null: rho(ΔO2, R*)")
)

df_obs <- tibble(
  rho  = c(obs_cor_OFR, obs_cor_DO2),
  type = c("Null: rho(OFR, R*)", "Null: rho(ΔO2, R*)")
)

p_null <- ggplot(df_null, aes(x = rho)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~type, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_vline(data = df_obs, aes(xintercept = rho), linewidth = 1) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Spearman ρ under null (product-induced)", y = "Count")

p_null

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Fig_spuriousCorr_permutation_null_osculum_means.png"),
  plot = p_null,
  width = 7.5, height = 7.5, units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)




```
